Slice Completed build work Verification Source
#1 deployed launcher-icon, default-search-chip, and Groups x2many grid parity release `gorp-3d3fa682` to production while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The running process and `/opt/goerp/current` both point to `/opt/goerp/releases/gorp-3d3fa682`, both public `/web` hostnames returned HTTP 200, and production browser smoke passed for default webclient action, Apps catalog, Groups form notebook/users grid, Groups list, Scheduled Actions list, and Users list with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_icons_groups_grid_3d3fa682/manifest.json`. Sartre was assigned a new independent verifier run against commit `3d3fa682`. Remaining gaps: independent verifier result is pending, Server/Scheduled Action form parity remains open, Users access form parity remains open, broader launcher/Apps/Settings pixel parity remains open, relation dropdown context parity remains open, mobile polish remains open, reference-only Apps installability remains open, and accounting remains phase 2. not recorded line 5
#2 closed a bounded part of the verifier P0 launcher-icon, default search-chip, and Groups x2many grid gaps while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The clean-room launcher now renders distinct generated Apps and Settings icon treatments instead of the shared cube fallback, Apps catalog cards receive module-family icon tokens, default search facets now cover `Top-level actions`, `All`, `Internal Users`, and `Internal Groups`, and the Groups form Users tab now renders an Odoo-like one2many list grid with Name/Login/Role columns, `Add a line`, blank rows, full-width sheet/body overrides, and smoke assertions for sheet fill and x2many structure. Focused frontend and HTTP tests passed, focused local browser smoke passed for default webclient action, Apps catalog, Groups form notebook/grid, and Groups list with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_icons_groups_grid_20260623_final/manifest.json`, and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: independent verifier still needs to rerun this slice, Server/Scheduled Action form parity remains open, Users access form parity remains open, broader launcher/Apps/Settings pixel parity remains open, relation dropdown context parity remains open, mobile polish remains open, reference-only Apps installability remains open, and accounting remains phase 2. full CI passed line 7
#3 recorded the independent post-change UI verifier result and routed the next implementation lane to Kant while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Sartre compared GoERP against fresh Odoo 19 Enterprise screenshots after the latest UI changes and reported required flows `0 PASS / 13 FAIL` plus supplemental mobile `0 PASS / 2 FAIL` in `reports/uiux/independent_ui_parity_after_change_20260623_074623/report.md`. The verifier confirmed that Technical dropdown behavior improved but still fails strict Odoo parity, and identified the next P0 blockers as fallback launcher/app icons, Server/Scheduled Action form sheets, Users/Groups access screens, missing Odoo search chips (`Top-level actions`, `All`, `Internal Users`, `Internal Groups`), and non-Odoo x2many grids/access layouts. Kant was reassigned those exact failures for the next implementation lane. Remaining gaps: all verifier P0/P1/P2 findings remain open until the next implementation and independent verifier pass, and accounting remains phase 2. not recorded line 9
#4 deployed UI parity release `gorp-45948387` to production while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. #5 tightened the strict UI parity lane for the topbar, readonly boolean fields, and Settings > Technical dropdown while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript webclient now passes the session database name into the navbar, renders an Odoo-like database badge under the user identity, replaces text debug labels with icon-only debug controls, renders inferred readonly boolean fields as checkbox widgets, and normalizes the Technical dropdown to the stricter Odoo 19 reference label set/order while hiding non-reference entries such as Users, Groups, Companies, Languages, Automation Rules, Apps, Scheduled Messages, Email Templates, and Incoming Mail Servers. The smoke gate now asserts the complete Technical dropdown label sequence and the launcher database badge. Focused frontend and HTTP tests passed, focused local browser smoke passed for nested Technical menus, open Technical dropdown, default webclient action, Apps catalog, Scheduled Actions list, Users list, and Groups list with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_technical_badge_20260623_pass/manifest.json` and `reports/uiux/main_technical_badge_20260623_boolean_pass/manifest.json`, and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: independent post-change verifier is still running, Users/Groups access form parity remains open, Server/Scheduled Action form parity remains open, launcher/Apps/Settings pixel parity remains open, relation dropdown context parity remains open, mobile polish remains open, reference-only Apps installability remains open, and accounting remains phase 2. full CI passed line 13
#6 confirmed the production report release is active with the same runtime code payload as `gorp-697eb0ee`, and rerouted the strict UI/UX parity lane with implementation and verification separated. Public `/web` checks returned HTTP 200 on `api.fadhelalqaidoomxyz.xyz` and `goerp.fadhelalqaidoomxyz.xyz`, and the server health endpoint returned `{"status":"ok"}`. Sartre completed an independent read-only Odoo 19 Enterprise screenshot comparison in `reports/uiux/independent_ui_parity_20260623_063912/report.md` and returned `0` pass / `13` fail. Kant was assigned the P0/P1 implementation goal from that report, and Sartre was assigned the follow-up read-only verifier goal so the implementer cannot self-certify. Remaining gaps: Technical menu exact order/nesting/dimensions, Users/Groups access form parity, Server/Scheduled Action list/form parity, launcher/Apps/Settings/topbar pixel parity, relation dropdown context parity, mobile polish, reference-only Apps installability, and accounting remains phase 2. not recorded line 15
#7 fixed the production Technical dropdown order drift found during post-deploy UI smoke. The clean-room base Technical menu now follows the Odoo 19 base order more closely with Email first, Actions second, User Interface before Database Structure, and Odoo-style labels for `Reports`, `Fields Selection`, `ManyToMany Relations`, and `Paper Format`. The browser smoke gate now checks the normalized Technical dropdown order and implemented phase-1 items instead of accepting a flattened local order. Focused base/http/runtime/frontend/smoke checks passed, local Technical dropdown smoke passed with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_technical_order_fix_20260623/manifest.json`, full `make ci` passed, production release `gorp-255d1b53` is active, public `/web` checks passed on both hostnames, and production browser smoke passed for Technical dropdowns, Settings, relation dropdowns, Scheduled Actions list, Apps catalog, and Users flow with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_technical_order_255d1b53/manifest.json`. Remaining gaps: exact pixel parity remains open, reference-only Apps are visible but not installable until real modules exist, relation create/open dialogs remain incomplete, broader mobile polish remains open, and accounting remains phase 2. full CI passed line 17
#8 tightened the UI/UX parity delivery lane while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Kant was redirected as implementation-only UI worker and Sartre as independent read-only screenshot verifier so implementation cannot self-certify. The TypeScript webclient now restores the correct app/menu context from hash routes, gives the Apps catalog Odoo-like navbar sections, sidebar filters/categories, 77 clean-room reference cards, Odoo-like app actions, and guarded reference-only module buttons so missing backend modules cannot trigger broken installs. Settings now keeps the Odoo-like General Settings surface with invite/user/language/company rows and cleaner inline invite styling. Users/Groups access-management views now show tighter Odoo-like list/form identity, smart buttons, access tabs, filtered internal records, and `Technical Documentation` group coverage. Scheduled Actions now have Odoo-like list columns, reference rows, run-manually form button, readable model/date labels, and focused smoke coverage. Focused frontend tests, frontend build, base/http tests, smoke-runner tests, and local browser smoke passed for nested Settings/Technical menus, relation dropdowns, Apps catalog, Apps install/detail, Users list, Groups list, Users flow, and Scheduled Actions with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_apps_access_parity_20260623_guarded/manifest.json`. Remaining gaps: independent screenshot verifier is still running, exact pixel parity remains open, reference-only Apps are visible but not installable until real modules exist, deeper Settings/search/mobile polish remains open, relation create/open dialogs remain incomplete, and accounting remains phase 2. not recorded line 19
#9 tightened Server Actions form chrome, `ir.model` relation behavior, and bounded Users/Groups chrome while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript form adds an Odoo-like `Create Contextual Action` control, adds a Scheduled Action smart button only when a server action is cron-backed, hides duplicate `model_name` fields when `model_id` exists, loads hidden cron metadata needed by the smart button, keeps `ir.model` relation dropdowns non-creatable, leaves readonly `model_id` openable/clickable for relation navigation, adds first-pass Odoo-style Users/Groups identity and smart-button chrome, and removes legacy gradient app-icon shell cues from the Apps catalog CSS. Focused frontend/index, HTTP/base, smoke-runner, frontend build, local browser smoke for Server Actions form plus relation dropdown, and full `make ci` passed from the clean release tree. Local evidence: `reports/uiux/main_server_action_form_chrome_20260623/manifest.json`. Production release `gorp-0f7964e9` is deployed; public `/web` checks passed on both hostnames and production smoke passed for Server Actions form plus relation dropdown with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_server_action_form_chrome_0f7964e9/manifest.json`. Independent verifier Sartre reran strict screenshot parity in `reports/uiux/verifier_strict_parity_20260623_current/report.md` and still reports `0` pass / `15` fail across launcher, Apps, Settings, Technical menu, Server/Scheduled Actions, relation dropdown, Users/Groups, and mobile. Kant continues the UI implementation lane from that verifier gap ledger. Remaining verifier P0 gaps: Apps catalog volume/categories/actions, complete Users/Groups access tabs/records, and native Settings/Technical exact order/layout; P1 gaps: Server/Scheduled Action data/layout parity, exact many2one autocomplete result cardinality, and mobile chrome/density. full CI passed line 21
#10 fixed the production many2one dropdown regression found during final smoke while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. `ir.model` relation fields are now treated as non-creatable at relation-config level, so Server Action model dropdowns no longer show `Create` or `Create and edit...` commands for technical model records; the regression covers both metadata and typed autocomplete behavior. Focused index/typecheck tests passed, local relation smoke passed with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_relation_no_create_20260623/manifest.json`, full `make ci` passed, production release `gorp-3d75843a` is deployed, and production smoke passed for launcher takeover, Settings desktop, mobile launcher, and relation dropdown with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_final_3d75843a/manifest.json`. Remaining verifier gaps: Apps catalog volume/categories/actions, Users/Groups access screens, exact Technical menu order/labels, Server/Scheduled Action data/form depth, and mobile form density/chrome. full CI passed line 23
#11 closed a bounded portion of Chandrasekhar verifier P0/P1 UI gaps while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript launcher now shows the Odoo-like root app set of `Apps` and `Settings` before search, while still allowing descendant app/action results during search; launcher smoke now rejects extra root tiles. Settings form actions now use the normal control-panel search area instead of an internal settings search panel, hide view switchers for Settings, preserve Settings search state through re-render, and keep the settings content aligned under the control panel. Clean-room CSS also tightens launcher width, Apps/Settings fallback icon treatment, desktop systray/user label visibility, and settings left-pane spacing. Focused home-menu/index/settings/main tests passed, local browser smoke passed for launcher takeover, Settings desktop, and mobile launcher with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_launcher_settings_chrome_20260623/manifest.json`; full `make ci` passed. Production release `gorp-bca64536` is deployed and production smoke passed for launcher takeover, Settings desktop, and mobile launcher with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_launcher_settings_chrome_bca64536/manifest.json`. Remaining verifier gaps: Apps catalog still lacks Odoo app-card volume/categories/actions, Users/Groups access screens remain incomplete, exact Technical menu order/labels still differ, Server/Scheduled Action data and forms need deeper parity, many2one autocomplete placement/results still differ, and mobile form density/chrome remains open. full CI passed line 25
#12 continued the strict UI/UX parity track with Kant as implementer and Chandrasekhar as independent screenshot verifier, keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Technical/action views now display human `ir.model` labels in lists, readonly relation fields, Server Action bands, and Automation bands instead of raw model strings such as `mail.mail` or single-token model names such as `delegation`; smoke coverage now rejects raw technical model values in the Technical list. Full `make ci` passed in a clean patched archive. Code commit `4b467377` was pushed and deployed to production after correcting release packaging to include generated `frontend/dist`, so `/web` serves the TypeScript webclient instead of falling back to the legacy inline shell. Production smoke passed for Technical dropdown, Technical search, Server Actions form, and relation dropdown with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_model_label_4b467377/manifest.json`. Remaining gaps: independent verifier returned FAIL in `reports/uiux/verifier_strict_parity_20260623_continued/report.md`; broader launcher/topbar/Apps/Settings pixel parity remains open, Users/Groups access screens remain incomplete versus Odoo, relation create/open dialogs are not full Odoo parity, and Settings structure still needs deeper Odoo matching. full CI passed line 27
#13 expanded clean-room Settings > Technical coverage and tightened relation dropdown behavior while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The base Technical menu now exposes 73 total menus with 45 added entries, 40 added window actions, 80 added generic list/form views, and 14 added system access rows; no accounting menus were added. Technical groups now include Actions, Automation, User Interface, Database Structure, Security, Email, Reporting, Parameters, and Sequences & Identifiers with actionable child entries such as Window Actions, Client Actions, Embedded Actions, Configuration Wizards, External Identifiers, System Parameters, Sequences, Paper Formats, Attachments, Assets, Logging, Profiling, User Devices, and extended mail technical records. Many2one dropdown cleanup keeps the Odoo-compatible `name_search` kwargs contract, highlights selected/current values, supports active keyboard selection, shows `Search more...`, and formats `ir.model` technical names as human labels where needed. The unsafe relation subagent patch was cleaned before merge. Independent verifier Bohr recorded the broader UI parity failures in `reports/uiux/goal_ui_parity_verifier_20260623_025703/report.md`; Avicenna closed the P0 Technical menu completeness slice. Focused smoke passed for Settings, Technical dropdown/search/form, and relation dropdown with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_technical_relation_final_20260623/manifest.json`; full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: Settings page structure still not full Odoo parity, launcher/topbar/app tile parity still fails, Server Actions native chrome is only partially improved, Apps catalog/kanban parity remains open, relation create/open dialogs still need deeper parity, and an independent screenshot verifier must rerun after deployment. full CI passed line 29
#14 tightened Settings action-link UI copy and styling toward Odoo settings rows while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Settings navigation actions now render as lightweight `o_setting_link` text actions instead of secondary shell buttons, and generic `Open ...` labels were replaced with direct Odoo-like labels such as `Server Actions`, `Automation Rules`, `Apps`, and `AI Apps`. The legacy and TypeScript smoke gates now reject generic `Open ...` action labels. Focused frontend test and full `make ci` passed. Fresh local smoke passed for Settings desktop plus nested menu navigation with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_settings_links_20260623_fresh/manifest.json`; production release `gorp-b6ea3fe0` passed the same Settings/nested-menu smoke with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_settings_links_b6ea3fe0/manifest.json`. Remaining gaps: complete Technical menu coverage versus Odoo debug mode, full Settings search/filter block parity, relation create/open dialogs, deeper x2many domain/onchange parity, and pixel-level UI parity. full CI passed line 31
#15 closed the production Groups form notebook/x2many parity gap and tightened launcher clean-room icon/background parity while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript form renderer now merges generated `res.groups` fallback notebook pages into the existing Odoo-style notebook, adds `Inherited By` only when `inherited_by_ids` metadata exists and is not already in the view arch, and requests `inherited_by_ids` in form `web_read` specifications. Regression coverage verifies the `Inherited` and `Inherited By` tabs render together and that `inherited_by_ids` appears as an Odoo-style many2many tag widget. The home menu now avoids generic glyph tiles for Apps/Settings so safe fallback icons are used, and the launcher background gate rejects old shell-like path/circle artwork. Focused frontend tests and full `make ci` passed. Fresh local smoke passed for default takeover, Groups form notebook, mobile launcher parity, and Technical dropdown open-state with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_groups_launcher_20260623_fresh/manifest.json` and `reports/uiux/main_technical_dropdown_open_20260623_fresh/manifest.json`. Verifier report is in `reports/uiux/verifier_strict_parity_20260623.md`; its G01 and G03 P0 findings are addressed in this slice, while G02 remains open. Production release `gorp-00598193` is deployed and post-deploy smoke passed for default takeover, Groups form notebook, mobile launcher parity, and Technical dropdown open-state with evidence in `reports/uiux/prod_groups_launcher_00598193/manifest.json`. Remaining gaps: complete Technical menu coverage versus Odoo debug mode, full Settings search/filter parity, relation create/open dialogs, deeper x2many domain/onchange parity, and pixel-level UI parity. full CI passed line 33
#16 closed bounded one2many list attribute parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Editable one2many lists now read create/delete/open-form metadata from nested list/tree attrs, expose that state as widget metadata, hide Add a line when `create="false"`, hide row Remove controls and the actions header when `delete="false"`, render readonly cells when `editable="false"` instead of inline inputs, and show an `open_form_view="1"` row Open control that dispatches a child form action. Focused webclient regressions cover field-level create disabling, nested list delete/editable/open_form_view attrs, and the row Open action path; full `make ci` passed. Local clean app smoke passed for Settings > Technical nested menus and Server Actions relation controls with evidence in `reports/uiux/main_one2many_attrs_20260623_local/manifest.json`. UI/Kant implementation work continues separately with an independent screenshot verifier comparing our screens against original Odoo. Remaining gaps: nested create/edit dialogs, deeper x2many onchange/domain parity, full Settings search/filter parity, and pixel-level UI parity. full CI passed line 35
#17 closed bounded Odoo-style Settings/Technical menu visibility, Users/Groups base-view parity, and row relation dropdown behavior while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript navbar now renders actionless menu groups as visible dropdown headers with all child action entries in the same Odoo-style dropdown, so Settings > Technical exposes Actions, User Interface, Database Structure, Security, Email, Apps, and all direct children. Added clean-room explicit `res.users` and `res.groups` list/form/search views with stable `base.view_users_form`/list/search and `base.view_groups_*` external IDs, wired Users/Groups actions to their list/search views, and kept fallback Users forms moving `group_ids` into an Access Rights notebook. One2many many2one cells now render searchable dropdown editors and serialize relation IDs in one2many commands. Focused frontend/navbar, base data, HTTP/view, smoke inventory, local browser smoke, frontend build, and full `make ci` passed. Evidence: `reports/uiux/main_nested_menu_relation_20260622_current/manifest.json`. Remaining UI gaps: richer per-privilege group matrix styling, relation create/open dialogs, domain/context/options parity, access-error states, mobile ActionMenus bottom-sheet parity, and deeper Settings search/filter behavior. full CI passed line 37
#18 fixed Odoo-like nested Settings/Technical navigation and editable relation dropdown behavior while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript navbar now preserves nested dropdown item classes during active-state refresh, renders category headers from direct-action semantics instead of inherited action metadata, supports richer Odoo menu payload lookup from both top-level ids and `children[id]`, and exposes route-safe menu opening for hash/menu-only navigation. The legacy inline webclient path now renders nested top-menu dropdowns with the same richer menu lookup. Editable many2one fields now expose a real dropdown toggle, open empty `name_search` suggestions without clearing the current selection, keep ARIA expanded state synced, and retain typed autocomplete selection behavior. Added focused navbar/home-menu/form regressions plus browser smoke coverage for Settings > Technical nested menu visibility and Server Actions many2one dropdown opening. Focused frontend, HTTP, smoke runner, and local browser smoke passed. Remaining UI gaps: deeper Settings search/filter parity, optional list columns/row selection actions, full users access-right notebook parity, relation create/open dialogs, and mobile form-control polish. regression coverage added line 39
#19 tightened strict clean-room Odoo 19 launcher and action chrome parity after the rejected production screenshot and Bacon's UI verifier failure. The TS home menu now renders a dismissible Odoo-like registration banner, starts with a dark generated wallpaper, transparent launcher navbar, Odoo-shaped app tile contracts, and Apps/Settings clean-room icon fallbacks instead of generic glyph squares. The smoke gate now requires a dark launcher background, visible desktop/mobile banner, transparent launcher navbar, 70px icons, and no mobile overflow. Enterprise-like action control panels now use neutral control-panel background while the navbar remains purple, closing Bacon's first blocker. Focused home-menu/frontend tests, HTTP shell tests, visual smoke for launcher/mobile/settings/apps/action surfaces, and full `make ci` passed locally. Current evidence: `reports/uiux/main_launcher_icon_parity_20260622/manifest.json`, `reports/uiux/main_control_panel_neutral_20260622/manifest.json`. Remaining UI parity gaps from Bacon: Settings search/filter behavior still needs full `.o_searchview` filtering parity, direct Apps hash route can still fall into generic kanban paths, list optional-columns/selection action flow is incomplete, `res.users` form lacks full notebook/access-right group parity, and mobile form/control-panel chrome needs cleanup beyond launcher. full CI passed line 41
#20 closed bounded ActionMenus keyboard navigation parity while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Toolbar hotkeys now open Actions with `u` and Print with `Shift+U`, ArrowDown/Enter/Space open toggles and focus the first enabled menu item, ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Home/End rove among enabled menu items, disabled/no-report rows are skipped, Escape closes and restores focus to the pre-open element, Tab closes without trapping focus, and Enter/Space activates the focused enabled menu item then closes the dropdown. Added fake-DOM `document.activeElement` support, focused frontend regressions, a dedicated `default-action-menus-keyboard-desktop` browser smoke scenario, and smoke inventory coverage. Focused frontend tests, smoke harness tests, frontend build, local keyboard browser smoke, full `make ci`, public HTTP checks on both `api` and `goerp` hostnames, and production keyboard browser smoke passed; deployed release is active under `/opt/goerp/releases/gorp-455133bb`. regression coverage added line 42
#21 hardened Odoo ActionMenus dropdown lifecycle while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Print and Actions sections now behave as one active dropdown group, opening one closes siblings, outside pointer/click, document Escape, popstate, and window blur close open sections, enabled item clicks close their menu, and print report validation loads before the Print menu is visually opened instead of after. Form action menus now live in the control-panel actions lane with mobile compact styling, hover-only dropdown display was removed, and focused unit/smoke regressions cover outside close, document Escape, sibling close, print validation call counts, mobile outside/Escape close, and action menu placement. Focused frontend tests, frontend build, smoke harness tests, local browser smoke, full `make ci`, public HTTP checks on both `api` and `goerp` hostnames, and production browser smoke passed; deployed release is active under `/opt/goerp/releases/gorp-46ac920f`. regression coverage added line 43
#22 hardened Odoo-like launcher/theme delivery after the reported stale dark production screenshot. The TypeScript webclient now supports Odoo-style app launcher overlay/back behavior from active actions, keeps the active action state behind the launcher, shows a back affordance in the navbar overlay, and adds strict browser smoke coverage for that lifecycle. Production `/web` now returns `Cache-Control: no-store, max-age=0` plus `Pragma: no-cache`/`Expires: 0`, and injects the webclient bundle with a content-hash query string so browser cache cannot keep serving an old launcher after deploy. Focused HTTP test, full `make ci`, production public header checks on both `goerp` and `api` hostnames, and production browser smoke for default takeover, launcher back mode, and mobile launcher parity passed. Deployed release is active under `/opt/goerp/releases/gorp-25bc0efc`. not recorded line 44
#23 tightened strict clean-room Odoo-like UI gates for the default TypeScript `/web` client after the rejected dark/custom launcher screenshot. The launcher now renders light Odoo-style home chrome with transparent home navbar, generated glyph app icons from safe `webIcon` metadata/default mappings, and no broken image/fallback overlay; normal action views now use a white Odoo-like navbar, visible purple app-launcher dots, light control panels, lists, settings sidebar, dropdowns, and relation widgets. Kanban QWeb template support also advanced for dynamic attributes, `t-att`/`t-attf`, `t-set`, named `t-call`, `t-foreach` loop context, inheritance patches, fragment rendering, and record fields not declared in the view. Focused frontend/HTTP/smoke regressions, local strict browser smoke, local kanban template browser smoke, full `make ci` passed, public `/web` and `/reports/progress_dashboard.html` HTTP checks passed, and production browser smoke passed; deployed release is active under `/opt/goerp/current`. Remaining gaps: exact proprietary Enterprise artwork/icons are not copied without provenance, deeper Settings/sidebar behavior, full search/filter/group dialogs, complete relation-widget dialogs, and broader pixel tuning beyond the verified surfaces. full CI passed line 45
#24 deployed and verified clean-room kanban action parity for non-accounting view workflows. Kanban records now expose record-level server action and print menu entries with active record context, grouped kanban cards support Odoo-like drag/drop between groups through ORM writes, grouped load-more regressions remain covered, and shell styling marks drag/drop state in light and Enterprise-like themes. Focused frontend regression, HTTP shell regression, smoke inventory, full `make ci`, local browser smoke, and production smoke passed with evidence in `reports/uiux/kanban_affected_local_20260622/manifest.json`, `reports/uiux/prod_post_switch_kanban_3f0d2cfe_20260622/manifest.json`, and `reports/uiux/prod_post_switch_launcher_retry_3f0d2cfe_20260622/manifest.json`; deployed release is active under `/opt/goerp/releases/gorp-3f0d2cfe`. Remaining gaps: cross-view kanban reordering persistence beyond group field writes, mobile drag alternatives, and full kanban quick-edit/card-template parity. regression coverage added line 46
#25 deployed and verified strict light Odoo-like launcher/core-shell parity for the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The production launcher now uses the requested light home action treatment instead of the rejected custom dark shell, has no registration/marketing banner, keeps Odoo-compatible home menu DOM contracts (`o_home_menu`, `o_home_menu_container`, `o_apps`, `o_draggable`, `a.o_app`, `.o_app_icon`, `.o_caption/.o_app_name`), keeps idle search hidden with keyboard-triggered overlay activation, preserves topbar/systray/company/user contracts, opens user preferences in a dialog, opens Apps detail, and renders usable Users list/form flows. Production smoke passed on `https://goerp.fadhelalqaidoomxyz.xyz/web` with evidence in `reports/uiux/strict_light_launcher_core_shell_20260622_production/manifest.json` and `default-webclient-takeover.png`; local rebuilt smoke passed in `reports/uiux/strict_light_launcher_core_shell_20260622_local_rebuilt/manifest.json`; full `make ci` passed; deployed binary is active under `/opt/goerp/releases/gorp-ce4bab01`. full CI passed line 47
#26 added focused browser regression coverage for Delegation assigned-roles one2many parity. The visual smoke runner now creates a bounded non-accounting Delegation fixture through RPC with explicit clean-room access setup, opens the real Delegation form route, verifies readonly one2many metadata, enters edit mode, verifies the inline `.gorp-one2many-editor` table for `delegation.line`, discards, and verifies readonly one2many restoration. Focused smoke inventory, diff check, and local browser smoke passed. Remaining gaps: one2many display-name polish for line records, many2one autocomplete cells inside one2many rows, nested create/edit dialogs, and full nested-list create/delete attribute enforcement. not recorded line 48
#27 advanced bounded clean-room form edit parity for normal form views. Generic form actions now enter edit mode for non-footer forms, render editable optional fields, support many2one `name_search` dropdown selection, editable many2many tag add/remove, bounded one2many row edit/add/remove commands, serialize many2one IDs, many2many SET commands, and one2many create/update/unlink commands on save, restore readonly relation widgets after save/discard, and style relation editors in light and Enterprise-like themes. Focused frontend form regressions, visual smoke inventory, HTTP asset regression, browser smoke on Server Actions and Groups relation editing, and full local `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: inline create/edit dialogs, access-error handling, full one2many widget/domain/onchange parity, many2many create-more behavior, onchange/domain/context parity, and full Odoo form widget coverage. full CI passed line 49
#28 advanced bounded clean-room launcher parity from the Kant UI goal. The Apps launcher now hides the technical parent path under the idle Apps tile, uses compact Odoo-like transparent app buttons with 54px icons, and visual smoke asserts launcher geometry/background/border metrics. Focused home-menu/index frontend tests, frontend build, visual smoke inventory, HTTP asset regression, launcher browser smoke, and full local `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: launcher notification/banner area, editable relation widgets, deeper Apps install/detail/wizard flows, advanced search menus, list/form density, kanban parity, mobile action drawer/search overlay, and live systray services. full CI passed line 50
#29 added bounded clean-room readonly x2many tag parity in form views. Readonly many2many/one2many values now render Odoo-shaped tag widgets with field/type/relation/count metadata, deduped tuple/object/id display items, sequential handling for SET/LINK/UPDATE/CREATE/DELETE/UNLINK/CLEAR command shapes, hash fallback navigation outside action-service contexts, and relation navigation for persisted tag records; shell styling covers light and Enterprise-like themes. Focused frontend build/form regression, command-order regressions, HTTP asset regression, visual smoke inventory, and local Groups notebook smoke passed. Remaining gaps: editable x2many list/tag editors, live populated x2many smoke fixture data, access-error handling, inline create/edit dialogs, and full relation widget parity. regression coverage added line 51
#30 added bounded clean-room many2one readonly relation-link parity in form views. Readonly many2one fields now render as Odoo-shaped `.o_field_many2one` links with relation/res-id metadata, route-safe `href` values, and action-service record opening that preserves context and replaces the current action; Enterprise-like shell styling covers light and dark themes. Focused frontend build/form regression, visual smoke inventory, HTTP asset regression, and local Server Actions form smoke passed. Remaining gaps: editable many2one autocomplete, external-link/open dialog variants, access-error handling, x2many embedded relation navigation, and full relation widget parity. not recorded line 52
#31 closed bounded clean-room Odoo form notebook parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client. Form views now parse `<notebook>`/`<page>` containers, render Odoo-shaped `.o_notebook`, `.nav-tabs`, `.nav-link`, and `.tab-pane` structures, keep notebook fields out of the main form grid without name-based data loss, support hidden pages/fields, preserve duplicate field instances when a field appears both outside and inside a notebook, and style tabs responsively in the shell theme. Focused frontend build, form renderer regression, HTTP asset regression, and Kant form/mobile visual smoke evidence passed. Remaining gaps: nested notebook edge cases beyond the bounded parser, exact Enterprise tab metrics without proprietary CSS/assets, and full form widget parity. not recorded line 53
#32 advanced bounded clean-room Enterprise-style UI polish for `/web`. The inline and bundled webclient surfaces now share dark Enterprise-like theme selectors for control panels, action manager content, list/form/settings/apps cards, dropdowns, buttons, search controls, launcher cards, and muted text; the TypeScript navbar now renders an Odoo-shaped user avatar/name split; the app launcher search is hidden until focus/typing while retaining keyboard search activation; and visual smoke style checks now accept the verified light and Enterprise-like palettes. Focused frontend navbar, shell, home-menu regressions, frontend build, visual smoke checks, and full CI passed. Remaining gaps: exact Enterprise component spacing/iconography without proprietary assets, complete Settings/sidebar parity, full list/form/kanban widget parity, grouped pager paths, and Kant's broader UI audit findings. full CI passed line 54
#33 closed bounded non-accounting immediate module data-load parity. Immediate install and immediate upgrade now run a post-lifecycle data hook that reloads selected modules' manifest XML/CSV data through the existing external-ID-aware loader, orders selected modules by manifest dependency, preserves the runtime root for release layouts, and keeps queued install/upgrade plus uninstall/cancel paths state-only. Runtime coverage proves reverse-ordered selected modules load in dependency order and resolve cross-module XML references; HTTP coverage proves the hook runs only for immediate install/upgrade. Focused lifecycle, HTTP, and runtime regressions passed. Remaining gaps: full registry/security/asset refresh after live lifecycle calls, uninstall cleanup of loaded records/external IDs, demo data toggles, update wizard orchestration beyond `update_list`, and exact Odoo registry rebuild behavior. regression coverage added line 55
#34 closed bounded non-accounting Odoo pager capped-count parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client. Window action list loads now send Odoo-shaped `count_limit = max(view_count_limit_or_10000, offset + limit) + 1`, list/kanban root `count_limit` attributes override the default cap, capped backend lengths render as `N+` with `.o_pager_limit_fetch`, limited next-page movement grows the effective count window, and clicking the capped total calls `search_count` with the current domain/context before rerunning the action with an exact `__pager_total` and omitting later `count_limit`. Search/filter/group/favorite/view changes still clear stale exact totals. Focused frontend build, control-panel regressions, and window-action regressions passed. Source/current audit agents confirmed remaining future gaps: editable pager ranges, pending-state duplicate suppression, first-page previous wrapping for unknown totals, backend count-decision optimization, grouped `web_read_group` pager path, grouped kanban pager hiding, and empty-page offset reset/reload. regression coverage added line 56
#35 closed bounded non-accounting Odoo pager fetch parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client. Window actions now carry page offset state, send `offset` with `web_search_read`, render the control-panel pager from the loaded offset, rerun next/previous clicks through the action service with clamped offsets, preserve active search facets during pager movement, and explicitly clear stale pager offset on search input, search suggestions, filter/group/favorite facet changes, and view switches. The control-panel pager now exposes the Odoo-shaped `.o_pager_counter` wrapper and guards disabled previous/next click callbacks for test DOM and real DOM parity. Source/current audit agents identified capped-count/editable/grouped pager behavior as remaining future work. Focused frontend build, control-panel regressions, and window-action regressions passed. Remaining gaps: capped `N+` totals and exact count fetch, editable pager value, `web_read_group` grouped pager path, grouped kanban pager hiding, empty-page offset reset/reload, route persistence for pager offset, and deeper list/search/control-panel parity. regression coverage added line 57
#36 closed bounded non-accounting module `update_list` parity. `ir.module.module.update_list` now scans the registered manifest map, creates missing rows as `uninstalled`/`uninstallable`, preserves existing lifecycle states, reactivates installable rows from `uninstallable`, synchronizes dependency rows including `auto_install_required`, synchronizes exclusion rows, deletes stale dependency/exclusion rows, returns Odoo-shaped `[updated, added]`, and keeps manifest data/demo/assets unloaded. The `base.module.update.update_module` wizard now calls `update_list`, stores `updated`, `added`, and `state='done'`, and returns `false`; non-source `button_update_list` remains unsupported. Focused module, lifecycle, HTTP, base, migration, and dashboard regressions passed. Remaining gaps: full live module data update/cleanup pipeline, uninstall/update wizards beyond this refresh wizard, registry/security/asset refresh after live lifecycle calls, category/country/module metadata depth, and exact source module install/update orchestration across dependency anchors. regression coverage added line 58
#37 closed bounded non-accounting module upgrade external dependency parity. Queued and immediate upgrade paths now expand installed reverse dependents, preflight selected/dependent module `external_dependencies` before state writes, include already-installed modules being upgraded, return installed immediate-upgrade plans for follow-up reload/data work, reuse the install-time Python/binary dependency checks, preserve no-partial-write behavior on missing dependencies, and keep cancel transitions unchanged. Focused lifecycle, HTTP dispatch, and dashboard regressions passed. Remaining gaps: full live module update/data cleanup pipeline, uninstall/update wizards, registry/security/asset refresh after live lifecycle calls, and exact source module install/update orchestration across dependency anchors. regression coverage added line 59
#38 closed bounded non-accounting module lifecycle external dependency parity. Manifests now preserve Odoo-shaped `external_dependencies` for JSON and Python manifests, including non-enforced `apt` package hints, install/immediate-install preflight checks declared `python` and `bin` dependencies before state writes, Python checks prefer distribution metadata before import fallback, dependency names are normalized/deduped, already-installed dependency modules are not rechecked, and missing target/transitive/auto-install dependencies leave module rows unchanged. Focused module parser and lifecycle regressions passed. Remaining gaps: full live module data loading/cleanup pipeline, uninstall wizards, registry/security/asset refresh after live lifecycle calls, and exact source module install orchestration across missing dependency anchors. regression coverage added line 60
#39 advanced bounded Odoo search autocomplete parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The control panel now renders Odoo-shaped `.o_searchview_autocomplete` rows from parsed searchable `<field>` entries, labels them as `Search <field> for: <query>`, appends a `Custom Filter...` row, hides autocomplete when the query is empty, and converts selected suggestions into field-specific text facets while clearing the pending broad query. Search facets now include the Odoo-compatible `o_facet_values_sep` separator class. Focused control-panel, search-arch, and window-action tests passed, along with full frontend lint/test/build. Remaining gaps: true many2one/selection autocomplete expansion, keyboard/IME/clickout behavior, custom filter/group dialogs, favorite edit/default toggles, mobile search panel, and deeper form/list/chatter parity. focused tests passed line 61
#40 advanced saved favorite management parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Parsed `ir.filters` favorites now preserve backend filter metadata (`id`, `user_id`, action scope, default/global state), control-panel favorite rows expose Odoo-compatible `.o_favorite_menu`, `.o_favorite_item`, `.o_favorite_meta`, and `.o_favorite_delete` selectors, user-owned favorites can be deleted through generic `orm.unlink("ir.filters", [id])`, active deleted favorites are removed from the rerun action facets, and success/failure notifications are emitted. Focused parser, control-panel, and window-action frontend regressions passed. Remaining gaps: favorite default toggle/edit UI, global favorite confirmation/delete policy, search autocomplete hardening, custom filter/group dialogs, mobile search panel, and deeper form/list/chatter parity. regression coverage added line 62
#41 advanced Odoo search-view parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Search arches now parse `<field name="...">` entries as searchable fields, dedupe them, preserve the parsed field list on `WindowActionResult.search.parsed`, and use only fields present in loaded view metadata to build typed search query domains. This moves global search beyond the previous default `display_name/name` fallback and lets Server Actions/technical views search the fields declared by their search view. Focused frontend regressions for the search arch parser and window-action execution passed. Remaining gaps: search autocomplete, custom filter/group dialogs, full favorite edit/delete/default behavior, mobile search panel, and deeper form/list/chatter parity. regression coverage added line 63
#42 advanced the legal OWL/frontend foundation and closed bounded activity/search parity gaps while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The frontend now declares `@odoo/owl` 2.8.3 as an LGPL-3.0-only dependency, exposes official OWL provenance and a runtime probe through the local `owl-compat` layer, and keeps the existing compatibility wrapper stable for current tests. Activity cards now refresh the navbar activity counter/dropdown after mutations and can open their related records through the local action service/hash route. `get_views(load_filters=true)` now returns scoped active `ir.filters` rows for the requested model/action/user, saved favorites can be persisted through generic `ir.filters` create, and the `ir.filters` record rule now matches the local `user_id` field. Focused frontend, HTTP, security, and build checks passed. Remaining gaps: full OWL runtime migration, exact Enterprise UI components without proprietary assets, search autocomplete/custom filter dialogs, full Discuss inbox/channels UI, activity edit/open-document parity beyond bounded record opening, and deeper form/list/chatter parity. not recorded line 64
#43 added bounded Odoo-style activity systray action handling for the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. `/mail/data` systray activity groups now expose `activity_ids` with focused regression coverage, and the TS activity action uses existing `mail.activity.activity_format`, `action_feedback`, `action_feedback_schedule_next`, reschedule, and cancel RPC paths to render `.o-mail-ActivityListPopover` cards with feedback textarea and activity action buttons. Activity refetches now carry the current session context. Focused frontend, HTTP, and mail regressions passed. Remaining gaps: exact Odoo activity menu group filter actions, edit modal/open-document action, live navbar counter refresh after activity mutation, full Discuss inbox/channels UI, mobile burger relocation, and deeper form/list/chatter parity. regression coverage added line 65
#44 closed bounded Odoo-style company switching for the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Backend sessions now persist active company context, validate unauthorized company IDs, support active-first `cids` cookies, clear `cids` on logout, preserve accessible companies separately from active `user_context.allowed_company_ids`, and keep switched company context across `/web/session/get_session_info`. The frontend company switcher now uses Odoo-like selectors, active/current ARIA states, local multi-company selection, Confirm/Reset, `.log_into` primary switching, search that ignores case/spaces, and posts both `company_id` and `company_ids` to `/web/session/switch_company`. Focused HTTP/security/frontend regressions and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: full Discuss inbox/channels UI, full activity edit/mark-done popovers, mobile burger relocation, select-all/ancestor-disabled company tree polish, deeper form/list/chatter parity, and broader visual smoke coverage for nonzero systray data. full CI passed line 66
#45 closed the static systray data/action gap for the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Bootstrap now fetches `/mail/data` with `init_messaging` and `systray_get_activities`, merges the returned `Store` into session metadata, and renders inbox, starred, and activity counters from backend data on first paint. The navbar now uses Odoo-like systray order, clean-room messaging/activity/company dropdown classes, live activity-group rows, current-company highlighting, typed menu action metadata, and user/logout/debug/company/mail/activity callbacks. Backend `/mail/data` regression now proves nonzero inbox/starred/activity counters from real unread notification, starred message, and activity fixtures. Focused frontend regressions, focused HTTP regression, full frontend suite, and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: full Discuss inbox/channels UI, full activity popover editing/mark-done flows, persistent multi-company switch route, mobile burger relocation, deeper form/list/chatter parity, and broader visual smoke coverage for nonzero systray data. full CI passed line 67
#46 added bounded default TypeScript Apps catalog lifecycle, mobile action-flow, and systray dropdown parity while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The default `/web` Apps entry now opens `gorp-apps-catalog`, loads `/web/session/modules`, preserves active catalog search across lifecycle actions, renders Odoo-shaped module cards, and runs install, upgrade, uninstall, cancel install, cancel upgrade, cancel uninstall, and restore through `ir.module.module` button methods. Direct module lifecycle RPC now requires admin/system-user context. Visual smoke forces `ai` through `uninstalled`, `installed`, `to install`, `to upgrade`, and `to remove` states, then verifies all visible lifecycle buttons return the expected state. Default mobile smoke opens Server Actions, renders mobile cards, opens a form, verifies breadcrumbs/form sheet/hash routing, and proves no horizontal overflow. The TS navbar now opens/closes message, activity, company, debug, and user systray dropdowns with Escape/outside-click behavior without changing the action manager. Focused frontend tests, smoke inventory tests, HTTP lifecycle tests, frontend build, full local visual smoke passed 23/23, and `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: Apps categories/filters/detail pages, confirmation/wizard behavior, deeper action/list/form parity, dialogs, real systray data/actions, and mobile search-panel parity. full CI passed line 68
#47 advanced non-accounting `ir.module.module` lifecycle parity while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Module install now preflights dependency closure before writing state, auto-install modules are queued or installed when trigger dependencies become active, exclusions block conflicting installs, uninstall rejects inactive states and active dependents, upgrade/cancel transitions are state-guarded, and `/web/session/modules` reports missing manifest rows as uninstalled. Runtime bootstrap now persists `ir.module.module.dependency` rows from manifests with `auto_install_required` metadata, the base model and migrations expose that field, and legacy Apps install calls `button_immediate_install` instead of directly writing module state. Focused base, migrate, lifecycle, runtime, HTTP, and shell tests passed. Remaining gaps: full install/update/uninstall data loading and cleanup pipeline, external dependency checks, uninstall wizards, registry/asset/security refresh after live lifecycle calls, and full `make ci`. focused tests passed line 69
#48 added normal internal-user `/web` parity coverage while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Runtime `res.users` records can now authenticate through `/web/session/authenticate` and hydrate the security user map with company, partner, and group metadata. The TypeScript and legacy launchers now show the Apps catalog only when the authenticated menu payload includes an accessible Apps menu, preventing base users from seeing install/apps entry points. Visual smoke now includes `normal-user-launcher-desktop`, proving Approvals opens as `approval.buttons` and Apps, Delegation, Settings, and Technical stay hidden. Focused HTTP auth tests, frontend shell/home-menu tests, smoke inventory tests, frontend build, and full local visual smoke passed 19/19. Remaining gaps: full `make ci`, apps install lifecycle, systray dropdowns, dialog lifecycle, mobile action-flow smoke, and deeper Odoo action/list/form parity. not recorded line 70
#49 wired default TypeScript `/web` control-panel interactions for real action execution while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Search filter, group-by, favorite, and facet-remove menu actions now carry parsed/fallback search facet payloads, rerun the current action through the action service with explicit `__search_facets`, and feed `web_search_read` with updated domains, contexts, and group-bys. View switch buttons now rerun the current action with reordered sibling views, update `view_type`, and clear stale `res_id` when switching back to non-form views. Focused frontend regressions cover filter toggle-off, group-by activation, explicit grouped `web_search_read`, form/list/kanban switch payloads, and kanban route serialization. Visual smoke now covers default TS Server Actions filter click and list/form/list view switching; full local smoke passed 18/18 and `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: persist search state in route hashes, live text search requery, custom filter/group dialogs, saved `ir.filters` writes, deeper breadcrumb action-stack parity, full form/list renderer parity, dialogs, apps install workflow, systray dropdowns, and mobile-specific search controls. full CI passed line 71
#50 added live TypeScript app-launcher search for nested menu actions so admin users can find and open deep Settings/Technical actions from the default `/web` UI. The clean-room home-menu model now indexes descendant menu names recursively, emits searchable menu-action cards only while searching, preserves root-app launcher behavior when the query is empty, and shows the parent path for direct actions such as Settings / Technical / Server Actions. Focused frontend regressions cover descendant action indexing, live search rerender, action card metadata, shell behavior, and app bootstrap. Browser smoke now covers the default TS Server Actions search/open path with route hash and 20 rendered rows. Remaining gaps: move Technical form/search-menu smoke from legacy to TS, richer Odoo menu sections/dropdowns, and normal-user Settings/Technical access remains intentionally blocked by `base.group_system`. regression coverage added line 72
#51 added bounded TypeScript target `new` modal rendering in the live `/web` action host while keeping accounting deferred. Window actions with `target="new"` now render an Odoo-shaped `.o_dialog` modal shell with `.modal`, `.modal-dialog`, `.modal-content`, `.modal-header`, `.modal-body.o_act_window`, close button dispatch, backdrop, and `body.modal-open` lifecycle without mutating the route hash. The action host now intercepts close actions, removes the top dialog/backdrop, preserves the parent action view, and avoids replacing the active view for non-window client actions after initial load. Focused frontend tests cover the modal renderer and app-level form-button wizard flow; focused HTTP asset/bootstrap checks passed. Remaining gaps: deterministic browser smoke fixture for a target-new wizard path, deeper dialog stacking/breadcrumb lifecycle, keyboard/backdrop-close behavior, and broader Odoo action-manager parity. not recorded line 73
#52 wired the clean-room settings renderer into the live default TypeScript `/web` Settings action while keeping accounting deferred. `res.config.settings` form actions now render Odoo-style settings app/block/setting structure through the shared TS renderer, show Save/Discard buttons in the control-panel button lane, track dirty fields, dispatch field/save/discard events, call ORM `web_save` for existing settings records or `create` for new transient records when available, and re-render original values on Discard. Focused TS tests cover dirty-state Save/Discard and settings renderer behavior. Visual smoke now asserts default `/web` Settings action renders `.o_settings_container` and disabled Save/Discard controls before edits. Local visual smoke passed 11/11. Remaining gaps: execute full `res.config.settings.execute` side effects, wire module-install flows, complete modal dialogs, deepen form/list/kanban renderers, and finish systray/mobile parity. not recorded line 74
#53 made the bundled TypeScript/Odoo-like webclient own default `/web` while keeping the prior inline shell behind `?legacy_webclient=1` for fallback and focused legacy smoke coverage. The default TS path now loads session/menu data, renders the Odoo-style navbar/home menu/action manager, opens app menu actions into the shared window-action renderer, writes route hash metadata, preserves menu labels as action titles, and has focused visual smoke coverage for default shell takeover and default Settings action rendering. Search/control-panel facets now carry category/value display metadata with multi-value `or` rendering. The action stack/router now track route metadata, target `new` dialogs outside the current route, target `main` stack clearing, current-route snapshots, active-id merge, nested dialog parents, and history-state actionStack payloads without leaking object arrays into URL hashes. The clean-room settings renderer is now wired into `res.config.settings` window actions with Odoo-style `<app>`, `<block>`, `<setting>`, typed controls, dirty state, Save/Discard buttons, and focused smoke assertions for `.o_settings_container`. Focused frontend/router/search/control/settings tests, full browser smoke, and `make ci` passed; local visual smoke is 11/11. Remaining gaps: real modal dialog rendering, deeper live action-stack navigation, richer Settings search/company-scope behavior, deeper form/list/kanban renderers, Apps install workflow, and systray/mobile parity. full CI passed line 75
#54 added bounded Odoo-style `/web` hash routing and restore parity while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The production inline web shell now writes route state for menu/action/model/view/record transitions using `#action=...&model=...&view_type=...&id=...&menu_id=...`, restores list/kanban/form routes on reload, supports browser back/forward through route restoration, clears stale record IDs when returning to list, and keeps menu-only routes stable. The TS takeover path now renders the shared Odoo-like shell from loaded session/menu data. The visual smoke harness now includes `hash-route-desktop` and `ts-webclient-takeover`, proving Server Actions route reload and bundled webclient shell takeover. Focused HTTP shell tests, smoke inventory tests, focused hash-route browser smoke, and full local visual smoke passed; local visual smoke is 10/10. Remaining gaps: make the bundled TS/OWL runtime the default web client, complete dialog target `new`, Settings typed save/discard parity, deeper list/form renderer parity, systray dropdown data, and full production smoke/deploy for this slice. not recorded line 76
#55 added a bounded `/web` theme/runtime stability pass while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The production inline shell now has fixed Odoo-style form control-panel layout so Save/Discard, breadcrumbs, pager, and form sheet do not overlap on desktop or mobile. The visual smoke harness now includes `technical-form-mobile` and validates form-header bounding boxes. A passive TypeScript webclient bootstrap entrypoint is now built under `frontend/apps/webclient`, served from `/web/static/frontend/` when a compiled dist exists, and injected without making extra session/menu requests during normal `/web` load. The `res.users` read path no longer mutates shared derived fields, removing the concurrent record-map crash exposed by browser smoke. Focused record, HTTP, frontend, smoke inventory, local browser visual smoke, and `make ci` checks passed; local visual smoke is 8/8. Remaining gaps: make the bundled TS/OWL runtime the default web client, complete action stack/router/dialog/settings/list/form parity, and ship compiled frontend assets in production releases. not recorded line 77
#56 wired parsed Odoo search views into the active TypeScript window-action path, excluding accounting modules. Window actions now parse loaded search arches and `ir.filters`, build default search facets from action context, merge default filter domains/context/group-by into initial `web_search_read`, expose parsed filter/group-by/favorite menu state on `WindowActionResult`, and render the Odoo-style control panel inside `renderWindowAction` with search chips, menus, pager, view switcher, and create button placement. Filter facets now carry `groupBy` descriptors through the search model. Focused frontend tests for search arch parsing, search model semantics, control panel rendering, and window-action execution passed. Production `/web` smoke verification returned `200 OK` and 7/7 visual scenarios passed. Remaining gaps: interactive control-panel requery/toggle wiring, persisted `ir.filters` favorite write flows, custom filter/group dialogs, and replacing the remaining inline production shell paths with the bundled runtime. not recorded line 78
#57 completed a fresh master UI parity audit for GoERP `/web` versus Odoo 19, excluding accounting modules. The new report at `reports/ui_parity_odoo19_master_next.md` records local and production HTTP status, local browser evidence for shell selectors, launcher, Settings, Technical Server Actions, search menu/filter/group-by, and mobile no-overflow, plus Odoo 19 reference selector paths used only for comparison. It prioritizes P0 gaps around replacing the inline production shell with the bundled runtime, real action stack/router/dialog targets, complete search model/control panel parity, real Settings form behavior, and full form renderer parity, then assigns independent implementation lanes for list, kanban, systray, dialogs, launcher, apps catalog, mobile, visual regression, and production selector smoke. Remaining gap: implement the report assignments in source. not recorded line 79
#58 added a frontend-only Odoo search-arch parser and grouped search semantics for the reusable OWL-compatible webclient layer, excluding accounting modules. The parser now converts `<filter>`, `<separator>`, search defaults, group-by contexts, and `ir.filters` rows into filter/group-by/favorite search items and default facets. The search model now supports date filter facets, date group-by descriptors with `year`, `quarter`, `month`, `week`, and `day`, favorite activation that clears current query/facets while applying favorite domain/context/group-by, and OR-within-group filter domains. The control panel now renders nested date/group options as `o_item_option`, exposes facet remove callbacks, and includes Odoo selectors for custom filter, custom group, favorite items, dividers, and the single `o_search_bar_menu`. Focused frontend tests passed. Remaining gaps: wire parsed search arches into active window-action rendering, persist favorites through `ir.filters`, implement custom filter/custom group dialogs, and mirror the same behavior in the production inline `/web` shell. focused tests passed line 80
#59 added a lightweight `/web` visual smoke harness for theme regression tracking, excluding accounting modules. `tools/web_visual_smoke` drives Chrome through DevTools, captures desktop and mobile screenshots for the app launcher, Settings, Technical Server Actions list/form, search dropdown, and mobile technical cards, emits selector-count assertions and SHA-256 hashes in `reports/web_visual_smoke/manifest.json`, supports exact baseline comparison, and documents CI usage. Verification generated 7 PNG captures against local GoERP on `127.0.0.1:18073`; all scenarios passed with no horizontal mobile overflow. Focused Node tests and harness inventory listing passed. Remaining gaps: add this harness to CI after the browser/runtime environment is pinned and add Odoo-reference comparison captures when a licensed/authenticated reference session is available. not recorded line 81
#60 added bounded Odoo 19 control-panel search dropdown parity and production release packaging guidance, excluding accounting modules. The shared TS control panel now renders one `o_search_bar_menu` containing `o_filter_menu`, `o_group_by_menu`, and `o_favorite_menu`, uses singular Odoo favorite selector naming, and renders structured `o_searchview_facet` chips with labels, values, and remove buttons. The production `/web` shell now exposes the same dropdown sections, active filter/group-by facets, persisted local favorites, clear search, filter domains, and grouped summaries through `web_read_group`. Local browser verification opened Settings > Users, confirmed Filters/Group By/Favorites, applied the Active filter, grouped by Active, then cleared back to the normal list. Focused frontend and HTTP tests passed. README release packaging now documents source-tree deployments so bootstrap fixture files under `internal/base` and `addons` are shipped with the binary. Remaining gaps: search arch parsing, date filter periods/date group intervals, custom filter/group dialogs, persisted `ir.filters` favorites, autocomplete, full OWL action-manager replacement for the inline shell, and screenshot regression automation. focused tests passed line 82
#61 completed the master UI parity audit and implemented low-risk Odoo-like UI fixes, excluding accounting modules. Parallel agents audited Odoo 19 reference selectors, current GoERP source, and local/production behavior. The report at `reports/ui_parity_odoo19_master.md` now reflects current production `200 OK` status instead of stale `502` data, plus prioritized gaps and next agent assignments. Local `/web` now opens Settings into an Odoo-like settings surface with settings blocks and action buttons instead of an empty generic list, form view now activates the Form switch and hides the prior list control panel, and empty list states use `o_view_nocontent`. Focused HTTP tests, local browser verification, and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: replace the inline `/web` shell with the bundled TS/OWL-style runtime, full action stack/search model/settings form/list/form/kanban parity, real systray/dialog services, provenance-safe app icons, and screenshot regression automation. full CI passed line 83
#62 improved Settings and Technical menu discoverability parity, excluding accounting modules. The `/web` shell now distinguishes direct menu actions from inherited child actions, so Settings still opens its settings action while Technical and category menus open a visible menu directory. App launcher search now indexes descendant menu paths and can open backed technical actions directly, including Server Actions, Scheduled Actions, Automation Rules, Email Templates, Users, Groups, Access Rights, Record Rules, Views, Models, and Apps. Menu payload regressions now lock direct-action metadata and requested technical XMLIDs. Focused base, HTTP, and runtime tests passed; local shell curl verification confirmed Settings is direct, Technical is navigable, and Server Actions/Email Templates are direct. Remaining gaps: true OWL action manager replacement for the inline shell, exact Odoo dropdown/menu hover behavior, real module install actions instead of state writes, complete search/filter/group-by/favorites model, systray dropdown data, breadcrumbs/hash restore, and screenshot visual regression automation. focused tests passed line 84
#63 added bounded Odoo 19 kanban renderer parity for the phase-1 web UI, excluding accounting modules. Source audit identified the broader Odoo kanban surface; local scope now covers ungrouped kanban rendering with `o_kanban_renderer o_renderer o_kanban_ungrouped`, `article.o_kanban_record` cards, `role="link"`, `data-id`, `o_kanban_record_title`, field rows, empty-state `o_view_nocontent`, kanban-specific create button marker in the shared webclient, and a visible `Kanban` switch in the production `/web` shell. Frontend regressions cover kanban action rendering and open-record dispatch; HTTP shell regressions lock the production selectors. Browser verification opened local `/web`, switched Settings to kanban empty state, then selected `res.partner` and verified two visible kanban cards. Remaining gaps: grouped kanban columns, card template compiler, card dropdown menu templates, progressbar/color semantics, drag/drop, quick create, load-more, arch `limit/count_limit/default_group_by/default_order`, and screenshot regression automation. regression coverage added line 85
#64 integrated the parallel phase-1 base/UI batch while keeping accounting modules deferred to phase 2. Added Odoo-shaped mail subscription routes, action-manager `default_get` behavior for create forms, OI workflow settings toggles, Eve/agent orchestration docs, UI shell selector/layout fixes, and the UI parity master report at `reports/ui_parity_odoo19_master.md`. Ran a live Odoo 19 reference on `127.0.0.1:8071`, verified GoERP `/web` launcher and Settings list/control-panel flow in the in-app browser, fixed inline search icon/caret layout, and split `internal/runtime` CI into bounded groups so the same runtime tests complete without long single-process termination. Final `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: exact Odoo Enterprise icon/background assets with clean provenance, full OWL action-manager replacement for the inline shell, real app install actions, complete search/filter/group-by/favorites model, systray dropdowns/counters, company/user menus, kanban renderer, mobile/sidebar parity, and screenshot visual regression automation. full CI passed line 86
#65 added Odoo 19 `/mail/read_subscription_data`, `/mail/thread/subscribe`, and `/mail/thread/unsubscribe` subscription route parity for OWL chatter, excluding accounting modules. Source comparison used `/Users/fadhelalqaidoom/Desktop/odoo/odoo19/odoo/addons/mail/controllers/thread.py`; local HTTP now returns Store-shaped follower/subtype subscription data, refreshes `mail.thread` Store payloads after subscribe/unsubscribe, defaults subscribe subtypes from matching default `mail.message.subtype` rows, and updates existing follower subtype preferences instead of duplicating followers. Added focused HTTP/mail regressions. Focused mail/http/base/migration checks and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: `message_get_followers`, composer post full Store response, scheduled-message post actions, full `mail.store` client service, and broader OWL chatter integration. full CI passed line 87
#66 improved web action-manager parity for technical/admin create forms, excluding accounting modules. The TypeScript ORM service now exposes Odoo-shaped `default_get`, and window actions opening a form view without a `res_id` now fetch model defaults for the active form specification instead of rendering an empty record. This enables Settings/Technical workflows such as creating a new `ir.actions.server` record to open with source-like defaults and context propagation. Focused frontend regression covers a new Server Action form action and verifies the `default_get` RPC arguments/context; frontend typecheck and frontend tests passed. Remaining gaps: full technical-menu action coverage, real module install action behavior, complete Odoo search model/dropdown behavior, and browser-level admin workflow screenshots. focused tests passed line 88
#67 added OI workflow settings-toggle admin-view parity, excluding accounting module implementation. The workflow module now exposes a `res.config.settings` extension model with source-shaped `module_oi_workflow_*` boolean toggles, registers that extension in the OI runtime/bootstrap metadata, and replaces the placeholder settings fixture with an inherited `base.res_config_settings_view_form` app/block containing workflow activation settings. Focused addon tests verify extension metadata and persisted settings view shape; focused runtime bootstrap check passed. Remaining gaps: actual install behavior for selected workflow submodules, deeper workflow runtime behavior, and complete Settings app UI parity. not recorded line 89
#68 added a bounded Odoo 19 Enterprise UI selector and behavior parity pass for the web shell, excluding accounting modules. Reference inspection used `/Users/fadhelalqaidoom/Desktop/odoo/odoo19` for class/layout names only. The shared TypeScript webclient navbar now exposes Enterprise-style `o_menu_toggle`, `o_menu_toggle_icon`, `o_navbar_apps_menu`, systray icon/dropdown, company `oe_topbar_name`, and user menu markers. The shared control panel now renders Odoo-shaped `o_control_panel_main`, breadcrumbs, main buttons, actions, navigation, `o_cp_pager/o_pager`, search input container, dropdown toggler, menu items, and view switcher classes. The production `/web` inline shell now mirrors those selectors for the app launcher, app catalog, list view, form control panel, save/discard buttons, systray, app menu entries, search controls, and pager text. Focused frontend package tests, frontend lint/build, and HTTP shell tests passed. Remaining gaps: true OWL action manager replacement for inline shell, full search model dropdown behavior, real pager totals from server count, exact Enterprise icon assets/provenance-safe replacements, systray counter data, company/user dropdown panels, mobile app sidebar behavior, form/chatter side-by-side layout polish, and screenshot-based visual regression coverage. focused tests passed line 90
#69 strengthened GoERP agent operating docs and Eve blueprints for ongoing parallel delivery. Added `goerp-agent-orchestration` as a repo-local skill covering lane ownership, non-overlap rules, Eve blueprint usage, verification matrices, dashboard handoff, and phase-1 accounting exclusion. Updated Eve platform and UI agents with current Eve filesystem surfaces (`tools/`, `sandbox/`, `schedules/`), deterministic helper tools for verification/UI parity targets, sandbox configs, recurring dashboard/UI drift prompts, and stricter UI comparison workflow against Odoo 19. Agent documentation checks now require the new skill and Eve tool/sandbox/schedule directories. Focused `go test ./tools/agent_docs_check -count=1` passed. Remaining gaps: run the new Eve blueprints in a real Eve project, add channel/connection examples when deployment targets are selected, and keep worker-generated DONE lines ordered before final dashboard regeneration. not recorded line 91
#70 added Odoo 19 `/mail/data` and `/mail/action` mail Store bootstrap parity for OWL chatter. The routes now process string and `[name, params, data_id]` fetch params, return raw Store-shaped data, resolve `DataResponse`, and support `init_messaging`, `mail.thread`, `failures`, `systray_get_activities`, `mail.canned.response`, and `avatar_card`. Thread bootstrap now exposes access flags, activities, attachments, followers, recipients, scheduled messages, suggested recipients, contact fields, and display names, with related `mail.activity`, `ir.attachment`, `mail.followers`, `res.partner`, and `mail.scheduled.message` rows. Added the `mail.canned.response` base model and migration plus focused HTTP regressions for Store bootstrap and `/mail/action` equivalence. Focused HTTP/base/migration/package tests and full `make ci` passed. Remaining gaps: full composer post Store response, `message_get_followers`, scheduled-message post actions, `/mail/read_subscription_data`, thread subscribe/unsubscribe, full `mail.store` client service, exact OWL Chatter component integration, and broader runtime regression sweeps. full CI passed line 92
#71 closed backend `/mail/thread/messages` fetch-window and Store payload parity needed by OWL chatter. The HTTP route now forwards Odoo-shaped `before`, `after`, `around`, `search_term`, and `is_notification` fetch params into the existing mail thread helper, so backend chatter supports infinite-scroll windows, jump/search counts, and notification filtering. Non-portal thread fetches now return markup tuple bodies, thread relations, reaction relation data, and related Store-style `mail.thread`, `mail.message.subtype`, `MessageReactions`, `res.partner`, `mail.guest`, `ir.attachment`, and `mail.notification` rows, while preserving raw tracking data and formatted `trackingValues`; `user_notification` rows are excluded and `is_notification=false` searches no longer match tracking-only text. Focused HTTP regressions cover ordered fetch, search/count, before/after/around, notification filtering, user-notification exclusion, tracking-search semantics, body markup, thread/subtype/notification store rows, reaction grouping, actor rows, and attachment rows; focused mail/helper regressions passed. Remaining gaps: `/mail/data`, `/mail/action`, full `mail.store` service, exact OWL `mail.Chatter` component integration, composer post full-store return, new-record save-before-post behavior, chatter layout placement rules, notification read-state parity in real sessions, full activity/follower/scheduled-message Store metadata, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 93
#72 wired Odoo-style form chatter containers to the real local `/mail/chatter_fetch` thread payload instead of a static placeholder. The TypeScript webclient now exposes the mail service through render options, passes portal/share access context, loads ordered `data["mail.message"]` rows, and renders Odoo-class message, avatar, author/date, body, attachment, and reaction DOM. The production Go-served `/web` shell now performs the same fetch/render path when a form view includes `<chatter/>`, with matching Odoo mail classes and focused asset markers. Frontend webclient regressions cover fetch arguments and rendered message DOM; the HTTP shell asset regression covers the production route/script markers. Remaining gaps: full composer send/log-note/activity flows, live bus updates, thread pagination/search, exact OWL mail component graph, many2one autocomplete/popovers, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, real module install actions, systray counters, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 94
#73 closed Odoo-style view widget rendering parity for the delegation-critical web surface. Source audit confirmed Odoo 19/18 uses `many2one_avatar_employee`, `badge` with `decoration-*`, list-root row decorations, `statusbar_state_duration`/statusbar Odoo classes, and `<chatter/>` containers for the relevant OI delegation views. The TypeScript webclient and production Go-served `/web` shell now render avatar many2one fields with Odoo avatar classes and image URLs, badge widgets with Odoo decoration/default classes, list row decoration classes, Odoo-compatible statusbar classes while preserving save behavior, and Odoo chatter container classes. Frontend regressions cover avatar, badge success/warning, hidden columns, row decoration, chatter, and statusbar class parity; focused HTTP shell/frontend webclient tests and full CI passed.
