Remaining gaps: real chatter message fetch/composer behavior, autocomplete/popover parity for many2one avatars, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget coverage, real module install actions, systray counters, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. full CI passed line 95
#74 closed OI delegation normal-user ACL, menu visibility, and group-pruned form-view parity. Source audit confirmed O18 grants `delegation` access through the `delegation_employee` security group, grants only `delegation.line` read access to plain `base.group_user`, and uses mutually exclusive `employee_id` form fields for system and non-system users. Runtime coverage now locks the source behavior: a base-only user receives access denied on `delegation.get_views`, the base-only menu no longer leaks the empty Delegation root, a `delegation_employee` user can see the Delegation menu and load the delegation form view, the non-system form payload keeps the readonly employee field and prunes the system-only active-user domain, and the system payload keeps the active-user domain while pruning the non-system readonly field. Focused runtime bootstrap tests and full CI passed. Remaining gaps: avatar/badge visual rendering parity, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray counters, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. full CI passed line 96
#75 closed OI delegation form/search/view metadata parity. Source audit confirmed the O18 delegation list/search/form views use `view_delegation_form_list`, `view_delegation_form_search`, and `view_delegation_form`, with employee avatar widgets, state badge decorations, active/draft/confirm filters, status/employee groupbys, revoke/statusbar header, group-specific employee fields, delegate and department domains/options, readonly date controls, assigned-role one2many list metadata, and chatter. Local fixtures now preserve that source-shaped XML, addon tests lock the raw persisted arches, runtime HTTP `get_views` verifies the workflow-mutated web payload including approval buttons and fields metadata, `/web/view/load` exposes the delegation views, and persisted DB action loads now synthesize `views` when stored `views` is false/empty. Focused OI delegation, HTTP action/view, and runtime bootstrap tests passed. Remaining gaps: normal-user delegation ACL/group-pruning matrix, avatar/badge visual rendering parity, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray counters, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. focused tests passed line 97
#76 closed OI delegation company-scope rule-domain parity. Source audit confirmed delegated record-rule domains evaluate as the delegator with the allowed company context narrowed to the delegate/delegator company intersection, and skip delegated rule domains when that intersection is empty. The Go security engine now builds a scoped delegator user for delegated record-rule evaluation, resolves `company_ids` and `company_ids + [False]` from the narrowed company set, keeps explicit row `company_id` constrained to both users, and locks the behavior with a regression covering non-`company_id` domain variables and empty intersections. Focused `internal/security`, `internal/delegation`, and `oi_delegation` tests passed. Remaining gaps: richer delegation form/search/view widgets, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray counters, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. focused tests passed line 98
#77 closed OI Login As systray/debug route parity. Source explorer confirmed O18 systray uses `LoginAs`, `LoginAsSystrayItem`, sequence `1`, dual `Login back`/`Login As` icon states, `session.impersonate`, and source debug menu replacement for `becomeSuperuser`; local assets now match those markers and remove the extra local `oi_login_as.open_login_as_wizard` debug item. Installed `oi_login_as` now enables the debug route, `/web/become/debug` defaults to `/odoo?debug=1`, already-system users redirect without creating an impersonation session or audit row, and `/odoo` renders the web client alias required by the O18 default redirect. Focused `oi_login_as`, HTTP, impersonation, runtime, and diff checks passed. Remaining gaps: deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray counters, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. not recorded line 99
#78 closed OI Login As wizard semantics parity. Source audit confirmed the O18 wizard has required `user_id`, optional visible-category `group_id`, computed visible sorted `group_ids`, readonly related `company_id`/`company_ids`, source form domains/context/widgets, `_onchange_group_id` clearing invalid users, and source `Switch to user` button text/class. Local metadata now marks `user_id` required and company/group display fields readonly/related, the form fixture matches the O18 source arch while preserving local compatibility fields off-view, record create/write computes related company/group values and rejects missing `user_id`, and HTTP `default_get`/`onchange` returns source-shaped computed values. Focused `oi_login_as`, HTTP login-as, full record, and source explorer checks passed. Remaining gaps: systray asset markup/order parity, debug menu route/default redirect parity, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. not recorded line 100
#79 closed OI Login As portal dropdown stop-link parity. Source O18/O17 `oi_login_as` inherits `portal.user_dropdown` with template id `user_dropdown`, priority `1`, inserts a `/web/login_back` menu item after `#o_logout`, gates it on `request.session.impersonate_uid`, and uses the source `fa-user-secret` icon plus `Stop impersonation` label. The local fixture now matches that template shape instead of the old banner-only extension, addon fixture loading persists the inherited QWeb view with the parent `portal.user_dropdown` external id, and runtime bootstrap coverage now checks the source-shaped `oi_login_as.user_dropdown` id. Focused `oi_login_as`, data-loader template, runtime bootstrap, and dashboard checks passed. Remaining gaps: richer login-as onchange/domain UI metadata, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. not recorded line 101
#80 closed OI Login As wizard route/action parity. Source audit confirmed `login.as.switch_to_user` returns an `ir.actions.act_url` to `/web/login_as/<user_id>`, the modal action is bound to `res.users` with `target="new"`, `default_user_id` context, system-group restriction, and the form footer calls `switch_to_user`. The Go runtime now dispatches `/web/dataset/call_button/login.as/switch_to_user` and the legacy local `action_login` alias, returns the source-shaped act URL, keeps redirect/group metadata for local extensions, updates the fixture action/view binding, and locks the behavior with addon/HTTP regressions. Focused `oi_login_as`, HTTP login-as, and diff checks passed. Remaining gaps: portal dropdown stop-link parity, richer login-as onchange/domain UI metadata, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 102
#81 implemented the P0 Web Shell, Home Menu, and Navbar lane for `/web`. The server shell now uses an Odoo-branded Enterprise-style topbar with launcher, mobile menu toggle, systray placeholders for messages/activities/company/debug/user, dark Enterprise home launcher, generated provenance-safe app icons, app search with Enter-open behavior, app/menu deduplication, module app-label normalization, mobile-safe body markup, contained mobile navigation, and mobile record-card rendering. #82 aligned OI delegation source install metadata so local `expired` remains an internal/runtime service state instead of a source-visible workflow state. O18 source only ships the delegation confirm approval button and does not define an expired approval settings state; local fixtures now remove the non-source `button_delegation_expire` and `approval_state_delegation_expired` records while preserving the internal `expire_delegation` record method, runtime expiry hooks, and HTTP/service regressions. Focused OI delegation fixture and lifecycle tests passed. #83 matched OI delegation `approval.log.delegation_employee_id` related-field parity. Source O18 declares `delegation_employee_id = related("delegation_id.employee_id")` and writes `delegation_id` from delegated approval records; the Go runtime now resolves the delegator employee from in-memory delegation services and persisted delegation rows, propagates it through classic workflow logs, advanced transition logs, wizard comment logs, mail-compose completion logs, and direct `approval.log` create/write normalization. Focused regressions cover classic delegated approval logs, advanced delegated transition logs, and direct record-layer create/write behavior. Focused workflow, delegation, record, OI delegation, and vet checks passed. Remaining gaps: source state alignment for local `submitted`/`expired` service-only semantics, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 105
#84 closed OI delegation state-update approval-log persistence. Source and local agents confirmed O18 delegation state changes create source-shaped `approval.log` rows through `on_state_updated`, with `record_id`, actor user, old/new state, date, description, duration seconds/hours, approval button, bulk flag, and delegation extension fields. Direct persisted delegation state writes now create fallback `approval.log` rows after existing workflow hooks run, update `last_state_update`, avoid duplicate logs when workflow dispatch already handled the transition, and use sudo for the internal log create just like source. HTTP `call_kw` delegation lifecycle methods now have approval-log regressions, and static base SQL now matches runtime approval-log duration schema. Focused delegation, HTTP, record, workflow, and migration checks passed. Remaining gaps: source state alignment for local `submitted`/`expired` service-only semantics, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 106
#85 closed OI delegation runtime menu resolver parity. Source and local agents confirmed delegated menu visibility must use `active_groups_ids` including direct, implied, delegated, and delegated-implied groups; `base.group_no_one` is removed outside debug mode; restricted mode filters delegated access to `restricted_access` groups; and action menus must pass read ACL checks on the action model. The Go runtime now binds a `delegation.MenuResolver` over the runtime menu/action registries and security engine, includes visible action-menu ancestors, hides missing or ACL-denied actions, removes `group_no_one` when debug is false, and loads the persisted `restricted_access` config flag into the delegation service. Focused delegation, menu, OI delegation, and runtime menu regressions passed. Remaining gaps: persisted delegation workflow-hook writes, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 107
#86 matched OI delegation source security/action/menu/cache-clear parity for the install metadata and executable Go runtime path. Source and local agents confirmed O18 source behavior for root/admin membership on delegation manager/admin groups, `act_delegation` with `list,form` and `{'delegation' : True}`, root `menu_delegation` with source web icon, daily root-user `cron_clear_access_cache`, and `_clear_access_cache()` cache invalidation semantics. The loader now accepts source `res.groups.users` as `user_ids` and derives menu names from linked action records; OI delegation fixtures now load the source action/menu/cron/root-admin memberships while preserving local aliases; HTTP `/web/dataset/call_kw` handles `delegation._clear_access_cache`; runtime registers the named `delegation_clear_expired_access` scheduler action; cache clear events and workflow hook tables are present in fresh and upgrade SQL. Focused delegation, data-loader, HTTP, runtime, migration, security, record, scheduler, and action regressions passed. Remaining gaps: delegated menu resolver binding, persisted delegation workflow-hook writes, deeper O18 delegated company-scope rule-domain parity, richer delegation form/search/view widgets, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views/settings toggles, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 108
#87 matched OI delegation date, overlap, active, revoke, and expire lifecycle parity across the persisted record model and Odoo-shaped HTTP methods. Source and local agents confirmed O18/O17 semantics: `date_from <= date_to` is an ORM constraint, self-delegation is blocked, submit/confirm rejects missing assigned roles and past `date_from`, overlap is inclusive for the same delegator/group unless `res.groups.allow_multiple_delegation` is true, `isactive` is true only for confirmed current delegations, and revoke rejects expired delegations before writing `state='revoked'`. The Go record layer now validates date/self constraints, computes `delegation.isactive`, syncs delegation-line related state/date/delegator fields, validates confirm transitions, exposes record actions for confirm/submit/revoke/expire, registers the local `expire_delegation` workflow method, and routes `delegation` lifecycle calls through `/web/dataset/call_kw`. Focused delegation, record, workflow, and HTTP regressions passed. Remaining gaps: deeper delegation source access/menu domains, source cache invalidation semantics, richer delegation form/list/search/menu parity, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. regression coverage added line 109
#88 matched OI delegation onchange/default field parity for delegator-driven line handling. `delegation.lines` now has the source inverse relation metadata, `delegation.default_get`/create defaults fill `date_from` and the current user's employee/user when available, `/web/dataset/call_kw` handles `delegation.onchange` for `user_id`/`employee_id` by clearing lines and returning create commands for the delegator user's `allow_delegation` groups sorted by delegation display label, `delegateTo_employee_id` onchange propagates the employee to line commands, and `one_employee` onchange clears delegate-to fields when unchecked. Explicit `delegation.lines` one2many commands now materialize into real `delegation.line` rows on create/write, including source clear-plus-create replacement, so web-save returns real child lines. Focused delegation, record, HTTP, workflow, base, and dashboard-tool tests passed. Remaining gaps: deeper delegation source access/menu domains, source cache invalidation semantics, richer delegation form/list/search/menu parity, delegation overlap/date/revoke constraint parity, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. focused tests passed line 110
#89 matched OI delegation mail CC and `res.users.write()` source parity. Direct `mail.mail` ORM create now consumes transient source `template_id`, removes it before field validation/persistence, loads `mail.template.delegation_group_ids`, parses existing `email_to`/`email_cc` with comma and semicolon separators, resolves recipient employees from both work emails and partner recipient commands, finds date-valid confirmed delegations, appends active delegation-line employee work emails to `email_cc`, and deduplicates deterministically. `res.users` writes that touch `active`, `groups_id`, or `group_ids` now deactivate current confirmed active delegation lines when the delegator user is archived or no longer has the delegated group, preserving still-valid group lines. Focused regressions cover raw `mail.mail.Create` with source `template_id`, duplicate CC handling, no-op templates without delegation groups, group-removal deactivation, and archive deactivation. Focused delegation addon and record tests passed. Remaining gaps: delegation source onchange line seeding from delegator groups, deeper source access/menu domains, source cache invalidation semantics, richer delegation form/list/search/menu parity, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and broader runtime regression sweeps. focused tests passed line 111
#90 closed bounded OI delegation model/schema source parity for source field naming and line uniqueness. The `delegation` model now preserves source `delegateTo_employee_id` beside the local snake-case alias and keeps both aliases synchronized on direct ORM create/write. Delegation metadata now marks source-required date/employee fields, readonly related user fields, indexed related delegation-line user fields, and source-shaped delegation-line required fields. Direct ORM create defaults `date_from`, derives `user_id` and `delegate_to_user_id` from `hr.employee.user_id`, fills delegation-line related fields from the parent delegation, defaults line `active=true`, applies one-employee delegate propagation, and rejects duplicate `(delegation_id, group_id)` lines. Fresh and upgrade SQL now expose the source alias column plus the delegation-line unique index, and delegation views now resolve `delegateTo_employee_id` and `department_ids`. Focused delegation, record, migration, workflow, OI workflow, OI workflow advance, login-as, and dashboard-tool tests passed. Delegation mail/write-hook explorer identified the next lane: `mail.mail.template_id` transient create-time CC expansion and `res.users.write()` active/group changes that deactivate invalid active delegation lines. Remaining gaps: delegation mail CC parity, delegation `res.users.write()` revalidation hook, source expiry/cache semantics, source security-scope tightening, root/admin group memberships, richer delegation form/list/search/menu parity, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, and mobile polish. focused tests passed line 112
#91 matched OI workflow advance committee transition source parity. Advanced committee transitions now use the OI source flow: non-superuser committee approvals add the actor to done users, keep the process on the current node while remaining approvers exist, finalize immediately when source `committee_limit >= len(done_users)`, and let superuser bypass the partial-committee path. Dispatch run-as-superuser prediction now uses the same remaining-approver rule so partial committee logs stay under the real actor while final elevated transitions use sudo. Focused regressions cover no-limit partial approval, source `committee_limit` finalization, and superuser committee bypass. Focused workflow, actions, security, HTTP, OI workflow, OI workflow advance, migration, dashboard-tool, and runtime tests passed. Delegation source explorer identified the next parity lane: preserve `delegateTo_employee_id`, required/related/unique metadata, `res.users` write-hook revalidation, mail-template/mail.mail CC expansion, related `delegation_employee_id`, source expiry semantics, source security scope, root/admin group memberships, and richer delegation views/actions/menus. Remaining gaps: delegation parity items from the explorer, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. focused tests passed line 113
#92 added OI classic workflow committee voting parity for `approval.config.is_voting`, `committee_vote_percentage`, and `approval.buttons.voting_type`. Classic approval buttons now load and carry `voting_type`, force approve action semantics when `voting_type` is present, and reject source-invalid configs that combine `committee_limit` with voting. Committee voting records preserve approve/reject/abstain intent with one vote per user/model/record/state; partial committee votes keep the record in the same state while firing `on_committee_approval`; final committee votes approve when the approve percentage is equal to or above the configured threshold and reject through the normal reject state path when below threshold, without firing approval/approve triggers. Vote persistence now writes the source-shaped vote value into `approval.log.voting`. Focused regressions cover below-threshold rejection, threshold equality approval, vote recording, forced approve action loading, duplicate-vote prevention, invalid config rejection, and trigger routing. Focused workflow, HTTP, OI workflow, OI workflow advance, migration, and dashboard-tool tests passed. Remaining gaps: advanced workflow source-vs-local committee edge cases, delegation record-rule/menu domains, `delegateTo_employee_id`, delegation mail-template CC expansion, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. focused tests passed line 114
#93 added OI workflow `run_as_superuser` parity for classic buttons, follow-up workflow automations, advanced transitions, advanced node actions, and advanced auto transitions while preserving the real actor user instead of replacing it with user `1`. The record/security/action execution contexts now carry a separate `Sudo` flag for ACL, field-group, record-rule, and server-action group bypass; classic and advanced workflow dispatch use sudo envs only for elevated writes; advanced committee partial approvals do not sudo early; and focused regressions lock actor identity, sudo bypass, restricted server actions, automation propagation, and security policy behavior. Server routing audit found Nginx active, `goerp.service` active on `127.0.0.1:8070`, `api.fadhelalqaidoomxyz.xyz` HTTPS working, and `goerp.fadhelalqaidoomxyz.xyz` blocked by missing Namecheap DNS A record plus missing TLS certificate. Focused workflow/actions/security tests, HTTP/OI workflow/OI workflow advance/migration/dashboard tests, and runtime tests passed. Remaining gaps: source `voting_type` committee flow, delegation record-rule/menu domains, `delegateTo_employee_id`, delegation mail-template CC expansion, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. focused tests passed line 115
#94 matched OI workflow advance `approval.state.update` wizard parity. HTTP `default_get` now computes `workflow_model`, shared `workflow_id`, and shared `workflow_node_id` from `res_model`/`res_ids`; onchange copies node state and clears incompatible nodes; advanced `action_update` stores `_old_workflow_node_id`, updates or creates workflow process state, persists workflow fields on records, returns source-shaped `soft_reload`, and keeps classic fallback returning window close. Focused regressions and workflow, HTTP, OI workflow, OI workflow advance, migration, runtime, and dashboard-tool checks passed. Remaining gaps from the latest agent audit: `run_as_superuser` execution parity, source `voting_type` committee flow, delegation record-rule/menu domains, `delegateTo_employee_id`, delegation mail-template CC expansion, login-as wizard method parity, fuller workflow admin views, workflow settings toggles, lifecycle hooks, true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. regression coverage added line 116
#95 matched OI workflow approval-log duration fields for classic and advanced flows. `approval.log.duration_seconds` is now source-shaped as a float, `duration_hours` is present in fresh and upgrade SQL, classic approval log writes preserve fractional seconds/hours, and advanced workflow transition/escalation logs compute `duration_seconds` and `duration_hours` from the previous workflow process update before node changes. Focused migration, workflow lifecycle, escalation, and OI workflow advance regressions passed. Remaining gaps: true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions instead of direct state write, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. regression coverage added line 117
#96 matched OI advanced workflow escalation calendar semantics for day-based escalation. The base model layer and migrations now expose Odoo resource calendar tables (`resource.calendar`, `resource.calendar.attendance`, `resource.calendar.leaves`, `resource.resource`), advanced workflow loading preserves `workflow.node.trg_date_calendar_id`, and day-delay escalation uses source-shaped `resource.calendar.plan_days` behavior against working-day attendance intervals before falling back to simple calendar days. Dispatcher autostart now carries the dispatcher clock into workflow start, making escalation dates deterministic. Focused base, migration, workflow, and OI workflow advance regressions passed. Remaining gaps: true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions instead of direct state write, chatter/statusbar/systray parity, breadcrumbs/hash restore, mobile polish, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. regression coverage added line 118

#98 tightened the Go ERP `/web` normal-user interface toward minimal Odoo Enterprise-style density. The webclient CSS now uses restrained solid surfaces with no decorative gradients, the app launcher uses Odoo-like `.o_app` cards, technical model/field/limit controls and manual load actions are hidden from the normal surface, list/form labels are humanized, and normal base users can open approval list/form views without approval-settings ACL leakage during view mutation. Browser verification with a seeded normal user confirmed title `Odoo`, `.o_web_client`, `.o_main_navbar`, `.o_action_manager`, app launcher, list view, form sheet, no visible `Gorp`, `Developer RPC`, `Build dashboard`, Settings, Technical, or access-denied UI, and screenshot evidence under `reports/verification/`. #100 persisted source-shaped advanced workflow transition metadata for OI workflow records. Advanced workflow state persistence now writes `workflow_transition_ids` beside `workflow_node_id`, `_workflow_transition_id`, approvers, partners, `user_can_approve`, workflow view, and activity deadline, using OI source semantics: only active user nodes for the current approver expose ordered visible transitions, filtered by transition condition, transition groups, active state, and hidden state. #101 added repository-local GoERP agent skills and Eve-ready agent blueprints for future platform work. The new skills teach agents the base/runtime/kernel ownership map, Odoo Enterprise-style `/web` UI rules, OI parity delivery loop, source-provenance constraints, phase-1 accounting gate, dashboard update workflow, and verification commands. The Eve blueprints define filesystem-first `agent/` directories for a GoERP platform maintainer and UI verifier, with instructions, reusable skills, and specialized subagent prompts matching Eve's `instructions.md`, `agent.ts`, `skills/`, and `subagents/` layout. README now points agents to these artifacts, and `tools/agent_docs_check` locks the skill and blueprint structure. Focused agent-doc and dashboard tests passed. #102 completed asset-backed `web.assets_backend` debug serving. The runtime asset registry now attaches a filesystem resolver rooted at the app source, filters to installed addons, normalizes manifest `static/...` entries to Odoo-style addon-qualified paths, and preserves exact path/target handling for manifest operations. HTTP now serves `/web/assets/debug/<bundle>/<asset>` for real bundle members with GET/HEAD, rejects non-members and unsafe paths, and keeps debug manifest URLs backed by files. Focused asset, HTTP, and runtime regressions passed. Remaining gaps: true OWL action-manager/service graph, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions instead of direct state write, chatter/statusbar widgets, breadcrumbs/hash restore, fuller mobile/systray parity, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. regression coverage added line 124
#103 added Odoo-style Settings and Technical menus as real base fixtures, exposed Users, Groups, Companies, Server Actions, Scheduled Actions, Automation Rules, Views, Menu Items, Models, Fields, Access Rights, Record Rules, Apps, and email technical menus, added technical list/form views and system ACLs so real admin users can create server actions and manage technical records, phase-gated the Accounting app out of the default runtime while preserving a `GORP_ENABLE_ACCOUNTING=1` phase-2 opt-in, pushed commit `dfd8413`, deployed it to the remote `goerp` service, fixed the build dashboard parser so newest-first backlog entries drive the live latest-slice and remaining-gap panels, and added a guarded `/reports/*` route so `reports/progress_dashboard.html` opens through the app. Focused base/runtime/http/dashboard tests passed. Remaining gaps: true OWL action-manager/service graph, asset-backed `web.assets_backend` boot, full view-arch widget rendering, real module install actions instead of direct state write, chatter/statusbar widgets, breadcrumbs/hash restore, fuller mobile/systray parity, and deeper OI workflow runtime behavior. focused tests passed line 125
#104 tightened `/web` so first load, app launcher, action list, and record form read closer to Odoo instead of a generic ERP shell. The webclient now exposes Odoo selectors/classes (`o_web_client`, `o_main_navbar`, `o_action_manager`, `o_app`, `o_control_panel`, `o_list_renderer`, `o_data_row`, `o_form_view`, `o_form_sheet`), hides the non-Odoo action sidebar and global diagnostic/status cues, opens records by clicking list rows instead of an `Open` button column, renders records as a form sheet with breadcrumb/save/reload controls, uses current action/model metadata for create/read/save calls, and normalizes action `_web_domain` / `_web_context` for Python/Odoo literals. Browser verification confirmed `/web` opens the Odoo-branded enterprise launcher, `Approvals` opens an Odoo-like list with two rows, row-click opens a form sheet, and forbidden shell copy is absent. Focused web/action/domain tests and full changed packages passed. #105 tightened `/web` theme parity after direct agent audit. The normal shell now uses the nine-dot launcher as the app switcher, removes generic topbar mode buttons, removes default Developer RPC and build-dashboard DOM, moves Apps installation into the app launcher, hides theme/model/field/limit controls from the normal surface, renames demo creation to an Odoo-style New action, removes app-card XMLID/debug spans, stops auto-loading module metadata during shell boot, and protects `/web/session/modules` when security sessions are active. Focused shell/security regressions and the full HTTP package passed. Running app checks confirmed `/web` serves Odoo-branded launcher HTML, forbidden shell strings are absent, and unauthenticated `/web/session/modules` returns 401. #106 hardened the `/web` theme into the primary Odoo-like testing surface. The shell now brands as Odoo, starts in an enterprise app launcher view, hides diagnostics/debug surfaces by default, uses single active workspace panels instead of stacked sections, exposes Apps/Records/Install Apps navigation, renders app cards without XML IDs or broken default images, switches app/menu actions into the record list view, keeps record forms inside the action workspace, and preserves standard/enterprise theme switching. Focused HTTP shell/menu regressions and the full HTTP package passed. The local server is running on `http://localhost:8069/web`. #107 revalidated Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI while keeping accounting feature work deferred to phase 2. Source/local/regression agents confirmed `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_id>` is public, bot-tracked, missing-message-safe, ignores incoming query params, uses default safe-method/unsafe-CSRF behavior, writes `link.tracker.click.whatsapp_message_id`, IP/country/campaign metadata, updates `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime`, derives `marketing.trace.links_click_datetime`, and triggers `whatsapp_click` / `whatsapp_not_click` child-trace effects. Local production route/model/schema code already matched the requested slice; no runtime code change was required. Added focused regressions for incoming query isolation, malformed safe-method no-side-effect handling, and source-shaped WhatsApp tracked-link field kinds/relations. Focused WhatsApp HTTP, runtime, record, base, and migration tests passed. Full `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 129
#108 closed bounded Odoo 19 `sms.composer` base wizard parity while keeping accounting feature work deferred to phase 2. Local metadata, static SQL, and versioned migrations now expose `sms.composer` with source-shaped composition, recipient, mass, template, body, and log-access fields. HTTP `/web/dataset/call_kw` now handles `sms.composer.default_get`, `action_send_sms`, `_action_send_sms`, and `action_send_sms_mass_now`. Mail helpers now support default recipient discovery, template/body rendering, direct numbers mode, single-record comment mode with edited phone write-back plus chatter SMS messages, and mass mode with duplicate/blacklist/missing/invalid cancellation rows. Added focused regressions for single and mass `default_get`, numbers sends, comment sends, mass-now sends, metadata, static SQL, and migration coverage. Focused SMS/base/migration tests and affected package tests passed. package tests passed line 130
#109 closed the requested Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity correction for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_message_id>` remains public, 301, bot-tracked for source-safe methods, missing-message-safe, and has no token/ownership check; local HTTP now also mirrors Odoo's default HTTP CSRF behavior by rejecting unsafe methods before click creation while accepting `GET`, `HEAD`, `OPTIONS`, and `TRACE`, and link-tracker click IP metadata now uses the request remote address like source instead of forwarded headers. Focused regressions cover safe-method tracking, unsafe-method no-side-effect rejection, malformed path no-side-effect handling, remote-address IP capture, click metadata, `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime`, marketing trace timestamp mirroring, and `whatsapp_click` / `whatsapp_not_click` child-trace behavior. #111 closed bounded Odoo 19 fetchmail cron progress and protocol-state parity. Source/local/regression agents confirmed Odoo increments fetchmail cron progress at initial server-count setup, per processed message, and per checked server; stops on remaining-time exhaustion; drafts stale-failing servers after the 5-day window without overwriting existing error fields; and keeps manual confirm limited to `state=done` on success with no error-field writes on failure. Local fetchmail now exposes a scheduler progress callback through server-action execution context, commits source-shaped fetchmail progress during cron runs, schedules immediate triggers when unread work remains, preserves existing stale-failure error metadata when drafting, includes `checked` and `remaining` in manual `fetch_mail` HTTP results, and keeps POP `STAT/LIST/RETR/DELE/QUIT` plus IMAP `SELECT/SEARCH/FETCH/-Seen/+Seen/CLOSE/LOGOUT` regressions locked. Focused mail/runtime/http/scheduler/actions/base/migration checks passed, including the combined mail/runtime/http/scheduler suite. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. regression coverage added line 133
#112 revalidated Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. #113 closed bounded Odoo 19 mail suggested-recipient and reply-all route parity. Source/local/regression agents confirmed the missing surface was `/mail/thread/recipients`, `/mail/thread/recipients/fields`, `/mail/thread/recipients/get_suggested_recipients`, plus inline `partner_emails` handling on `/mail/message/post`. Local mail now computes source-shaped suggested recipients from default record partners, primary email fields, responsible users, reply message authors/partners, filtered inbound `incoming_email_to`/`incoming_email_cc`, and `email_from`; creates partners when the route calls `no_create=False`; preserves raw email suggestions when `no_create=True`; excludes followers and alias-domain/mail-alias addresses; and exposes partner/primary-email metadata. HTTP routes now match the Odoo route shapes, and `partner_emails` are converted into partners before message posting/notifications. Added focused HTTP regressions for reply-all partner creation/exclusion, frontend-updated suggested recipients, recipient fields, and `partner_emails` side effects. #114 closed the requested Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity recheck for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. #115 closed Odoo 19 digest route parity for public unsubscribe and manager periodicity handling. Source/local/regression agents confirmed `/digest/<id>/unsubscribe_oneclik` is POST-only, public, token-protected, typo-preserving, and returns empty 200; `/digest/<id>/unsubscribe` supports GET/POST token unsubscribe, legacy logged internal-user unsubscribe, deprecated `one_click` GET rejection, 404 bad-token behavior, and success HTML with digest name; `/digest/<id>/set_periodicity` is authenticated, requires `base.group_erp_manager`, accepts `daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly`, writes `periodicity`, and redirects 303 to `/odoo/digest.digest/<id>`. Local HTTP now registers `/digest/`, validates digest HMAC tokens with constant-time comparison, updates `digest.digest.user_ids`, preserves source status behavior, and exposes bounded `digest.digest.action_set_periodicity` through `call_kw`. Added focused HTTP regressions for valid/invalid token unsubscribe, one-click POST-only behavior, legacy internal unsubscribe, manager periodicity auth/validation, and call_kw periodicity dispatch. #118 added Odoo 19 enterprise AI tool actions for fields/search/read_group/menu/report/open-menu/adjust-search, source-compatible topic bindings, default-agent topic wiring, runtime Go handlers, chat topic-tool resolution, allowlist filtering, provider tool-loop tests, and runtime bootstrap parity tests. not recorded line 148
#119 wired AI open-menu and adjust-search tools to source-shaped user bus events, added `/bus/poll`, propagated AI session identifiers into tool metadata, made draft-channel creation positional-compatible, and added frontend pure handlers for natural-language menu/search event payloads. not recorded line 149
#120 added frontend Odoo 19 AI draft-channel positional ORM helpers, draft result normalization, prompt-button storage helpers, Ask AI ORM helpers, record-context extraction, and `agent_chat_action` handling for invocation and action-registry call signatures. not recorded line 150
#121 regenerated Odoo 19 accounting inventory to include additional enterprise/community account modules visible under `/Users/fadhelalqaidoom/Desktop/odoo/odoo19`, including `account_transfer`, `account_fiscal_categories`, `account_online_payment`, `account_extract`, `account_iso20022`, `account_budget_purchase`, and `accountant`. not recorded line 162
#122 aligned `addons/accounting`, `internal/accounting`, and migrations on one canonical model registry with schema/registry guard tests. not recorded line 163
#123 replaced generated accounting ACLs with explicit Odoo-named ACL records for the local accounting registry and kept local-only model ACLs explicitly named. not recorded line 165
#124 aligned accounting runtime group constants with Odoo `group_account_readonly`, `group_account_invoice`, `group_account_basic`, `group_account_user`, and `group_account_manager`, with compatibility aliases for old local groups. not recorded line 166
#125 replaced generic global company-tree accounting rules with source-named rule definitions for moves, move lines, payments, journals, accounts, taxes, bank statements, payment terms, reconciliation models, reports, readonly access, and invoice access. not recorded line 167
#126 added table-driven ACL/group/rule characterization tests for accounting security. not recorded line 168
#127 aligned portal invoice and invoice-line record rules with Odoo 19 commercial-partner hierarchy semantics; draft/cancelled moves and receipt move types are denied. not recorded line 169
#128 added clean-room loadable accounting manifest fixtures for declared security, data, view, action, menu, report, mail-template, onboarding, tour, sequence, and generic chart paths. not recorded line 170
#129 added accounting manifest fixture existence/load coverage and persisted-security smoke coverage. not recorded line 171
#130 expanded `/web/dataset/call_kw` allowlist with `fields_get`, `default_get`, `name_get`, `name_search`, `web_name_search`, `search_count`, `web_read`, `web_search_read`, `web_save`, `web_save_multi`, and minimal `onchange`. not recorded line 201
#131 added model metadata helpers, default lookup from context, display-name/name-search helpers, search count limits, and web-read formatting. not recorded line 202
#132 added Odoo-style `ir.model.access` and `ir.rule` model/migration fields, persisted ACL/rule loading, `model_id` resolution, group/per-operation rule semantics, and CSV `*:id`/boolean parsing for access files. not recorded line 203
#133 added XML data loader `eval`, `search`, `ref(...)`, scalar/list/dict parsing, x2many `(4, id)` and `(6, 0, ids)` commands, and JSON serialization for text-field collection evals. not recorded line 204
#134 implemented external-ID update/noupdate overwrite semantics and loaded clean-room base security fixtures. not recorded line 205
#135 added bounded `/web/dataset/call_kw` `read_group` with simple scalar groupby, `__count`, merged `__domain`, and direct `record.ModelSet.ReadGroup`. not recorded line 211
#136 aligned `addons/accounting`, `internal/accounting`, and migrations on one canonical model registry and added schema/registry parity tests. not recorded line 219
#137 replaced broad generated accounting ACLs/rules with explicit Odoo-named group, ACL, and rule definitions for implemented local models. not recorded line 220
#138 added manifest file-existence/load tests and generated clean-room local accounting data files for every declared path. not recorded line 221
#139 added account move sequence-prefix/number, secure sequence number, gap detection, restricted-journal hash gating, previous-hash chaining, draft/cancel protections, hard/sale/purchase lock checks, and protected posted-field immutability tests. not recorded line 222
#140 aligned Odoo 19 portal invoice security for moves and move lines, including `child_of user.commercial_partner_id.id` on `partner_id`, local move-line denormalized fields, and receipt exclusion. not recorded line 223
#141 added bounded Odoo 19 `mail.template._process_attachments_for_template_post` parity. Template sends now run a postprocess attachment hook after static/report attachment preparation, direct template sends propagate hook attachments to both `mail.message` and `mail.mail`, scheduled template sends copy hook attachments onto `mail.scheduled.message`, and accounting send copies hook attachments onto the outgoing message/mail. The hook covers local/source-backed `account.move` UBL/CII XML attachments linked by `res_field=ubl_cii_xml_file`, plus future-ready `account.payment.l10n_mx_edi_cfdi_attachment_id` when that localization field exists. Source/local audit agents were closed. Focused and affected package tests plus `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 230
#142 added Odoo 19 `ir.actions.report.attachment` / `attachment_use` cache parity for dynamic template reports. Template report generation now evaluates `attachment` separately from `print_report_name`, stores source-record-owned report cache attachments by `name`/`res_model`/`res_id`, reuses cached bytes only when `attachment_use` is true, preserves existing caches without overwrite when `attachment_use` is false, honors `report_pdf_no_attachment`, and still creates target-owned `mail.message` / `mail.scheduled.message` attachments for outgoing mail. Accounting send inherits the same non-invoice dynamic report cache behavior. Source/local audit agents were closed. Focused and affected package tests plus `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 231
#143 added Odoo 19 restricted audit-trail attachment parity. `res.company.restrictive_audit_trail`, `ir.attachment.company_id`, and `account.move.ubl_cii_xml_file` are now model/schema/migration-backed. Company restrictive audit trail now feeds record-layer lock policies, and `posted_before` moves cannot be unlinked under restrictive audit trail. Direct `ir.attachment` writes/unlinks now detach first official invoice PDF/XML attachments, preserve content, block second unlink/content/ownership mutation for protected PDF/XML audit rows, and keep Odoo's documents-versioning relink exception. Source/local audit agents were closed. Affected package tests and `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 232
#144 added Odoo 19 invoice PDF reset/cancel/unlink parity. Account-move state writes now detach official invoice PDFs for posted/cancel reset and posted cancel paths from the record layer, clear stored invoice PDF fields, preserve the attachment row/content, and rename it with Odoo-style detached metadata. Generic `call_kw write({"state":"draft"})` now follows the same behavior as button reset. Account-move unlink removes linked official PDF attachments, invoice attachment downloads verify the invoice still exists, and stale/wrong stored `invoice_pdf_report_id` values no longer reassign unrelated attachments. Source/local audit agents were closed. Focused and affected package tests passed. package tests passed line 233
#145 added Odoo 19 invoice PDF binary-field attachment parity. `ir.attachment.res_field` is now model/schema/migration-backed; invoice PDF placeholders create and reuse `account.move` attachments with `res_field=invoice_pdf_report_file`, backfill existing PDF attachment metadata/content/file size, and sync `invoice_pdf_report_id`, `invoice_pdf_report_file`, and `message_main_attachment_id`. Mail/message copies keep empty `res_field`. Invoice attachment downloads now require invoice-PDF ownership. Static SQL now includes attachment checksum, thumbnail, res-field, and file-size parity. Source/local audit agents were closed. Affected package tests and `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 234
#146 added Odoo 19 `mail.template.report_template_ids` parity for dynamic report attachments. Template sends now create per-record `ir.attachment` rows owned by `mail.message`, preserve static template attachments by link, render common `ir.actions.report.print_report_name` object expressions, append format extensions once, and propagate generated attachments to `mail.message`, `mail.mail`, and `mail.scheduled.message`. Accounting send now appends non-invoice dynamic reports from the selected template while excluding invoice report duplicates already represented by `account.move.invoice_pdf_report_id`. Added model/schema/migration coverage plus mail and accounting regressions. Source/local audit agents were closed. Affected package tests and `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 235
#147 added Odoo 19 composer attachment-copy and bounded accounting send widget/template parity. `SendComposeMessages` and `ScheduleComposeMessages` now copy composer/template attachments per target message or scheduled row instead of sharing attachment IDs; direct `mail.template.send_mail` still preserves static template-owned attachments. Account send default_get now hydrates the default invoice mail template, rendered subject/body, partner recipients, display flag, and attachment widget with invoice placeholder/existing PDF plus static template attachments. Account send action now consumes `mail_attachments_widget`, honors skipped non-manual rows by name, preserves manual same-name rows, deduplicates selected attachment IDs, and creates message-owned copies linked to `mail.message` and `mail.mail`. Added focused mail and HTTP regressions. Source/local audit agents were closed. Affected package tests and `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 236
#148 added Odoo 19 static mail-template attachment parity and invoice PDF mail-copy parity. `mail.template.attachment_ids` now loads into template sends, compose defaults, `mail.message`, `mail.mail`, and `mail.scheduled.message`; direct template sends preserve template-owned attachment rows instead of relinking them to target records. Account invoice send now keeps `account.move.invoice_pdf_report_id` as the invoice-owned PDF and creates a message-owned PDF copy linked to both `mail.message` and `mail.mail`, matching Odoo's raw tuple/message-attachment behavior. Added focused mail and HTTP regressions. Source/local audit agents were closed. `go test ./internal/mail ./internal/http ./internal/base` and `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 237
#149 added Odoo 19 queue attachment send parity for body-linked, URL-only, and oversized record-owned attachments. Queue delivery now defers attachment materialization until after SMTP server selection, removes attachments already referenced by `/web/content/<id>` or `/web/image/<id>` in the HTML body, converts URL-only `http://`/`https://`/`ftp://` attachments to `/web/content/<id>?download=1&access_token=<token>` body links, converts oversized non-`mail.message` record-owned attachments to body links using selected-server/default max email size, reuses or creates persistent UUID4 access tokens, sorts remaining MIME attachments by ID, and adds `ir.attachment.file_size` to model/schema metadata for Odoo-sized estimates. Added focused queue regressions for linked-body dedupe, URL-only link conversion, and oversize record-owned link conversion. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 238
#150 added Odoo 19 per-record alias-domain delivery parity. `mail.message` and `mail.mail` now expose `record_alias_domain_id` and `record_company_id`; `PostMessage` stores the linked record company and alias domain using Odoo's `company_id`/env-company/first-domain fallback; queue sending resolves per-mail alias-domain context from explicit mail fields, linked message fields, or linked record company; SMTP server selection uses the record-domain default sender; Return-Path and envelope sender use the record-domain bounce address; personal-server split copies preserve record routing fields. Added regressions for persisted message alias domains, linked-record company alias domains, and message-post field persistence. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 239
#151 added Odoo 19 personal mail-server multi-minute bucket packing and mail queue cron retriggering. Personal-server throttling now keeps source-style future minute buckets for delayed mails, splits oversized partner-recipient rows while scheduling remaining recipients into the next available bucket, delays subsequent same-run mails into later buckets, and creates an `ir.cron.trigger` for `mail.ir_cron_mail_scheduler_action` at the earliest delayed minute plus 59 seconds. Added focused queue regressions for raw overflow bucket packing, split overflow plus subsequent delayed rows, and cron trigger creation. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 240
#152 added Odoo 19 `mail.mail.mail_server_id` create/write ownership constraints. Record create/write now allows public forced servers, allows personal forced servers only when `owner_user_id` equals linked `mail.message.create_uid`, rejects personal servers when `mail.disable_personal_mail_servers` is set, revalidates on `mail_server_id`/`mail_message_id` writes, and leaves send-only checks for active state, exact `from_filter`, and owner outgoing server in queue delivery. Added focused record regressions plus queue tests adjusted to keep delivery-time rejection coverage. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 241
#153 added bounded Odoo log-access and personal-server creator authority for mail-critical models. `mail.message`, `mail.mail`, and `mail.notification` now expose `create_uid`, `create_date`, `write_uid`, and `write_date`; record create/write populates declared audit fields from context and strips caller-supplied audit spoofing; mail personal-server authorization now uses linked `mail.message.create_uid`; split mail copies are created as the original mail creator; and due queue ordering now uses `create_date,id`. not recorded line 242
#154 added bounded Odoo-style personal-server overflow split/copy behavior. When partner-recipient quota exceeds the current minute's remaining capacity, queue delivery now creates a send-now `mail.mail` copy with the first allowed recipients, preserves raw To/Cc, headers, message, attachments, server, retry, and notification metadata, clears raw To/Cc on the delayed original, keeps the remaining recipients on the original, schedules it for the next minute, caps the server minute counter, and reassigns pending email notifications for the copied recipients/raw addresses to the copied mail. not recorded line 243
#155 added bounded personal mail-server throttle and stricter forced personal-server validation. Queue sending now validates forced personal servers on nil and custom sender paths, rejects inactive forced servers, requires exact personal `from_filter` email plus the owner's current outgoing server, rejects author spoofing without that current owner server, increments `owner_limit_count` per minute, resets old/future `owner_limit_time`, and delays over-limit outgoing rows. not recorded line 244
#156 added bounded Return-Path/envelope parity and fetchmail metadata surface. `Message.EnvelopeFrom` now lets SMTP `MAIL FROM` differ from header `From`; queue delivery sets `Return-Path` from context/company alias-domain/config bounce values, uses bounce envelope only when the selected server `from_filter` allows it, and preserves notification/default `From` behavior. `fetchmail.server` now exposes Odoo-shaped server/login/state/error/action fields in model metadata, baseline SQL, additive migration, and HTTP button RPCs for confirm, reset, and fetch timestamp clearing. not recorded line 245
#157 added Odoo-shaped mail server ownership and notification-address fallback for queue delivery. Schema/model/migrations now expose `ir.mail_server.owner_user_id`/owner limits, user outgoing-server fields, `mail.alias.domain`, `mail.alias` alias-domain fields, and `res.company.alias_domain_id`. Default SMTP selection excludes personal servers, parses comma-separated `from_filter`, checks sender/notification exact and domain matches, falls back to configured/default notification From, and rejects unauthorized forced personal servers while allowing author-owned personal sends. not recorded line 246
#158 added Odoo-shaped `mail.mail` recipient splitting and partial-success postprocess: raw To/Cc delivery is separated from partner-specific `recipient_ids`, Cc-only delivery is accepted, partner-only compose/template sends keep recipients out of raw `email_to`, successful partner/raw notifications are marked sent, failed unsent notifications remain exception, and missing vs invalid recipient failure types match source behavior. not recorded line 247
#159 added bounded persistent `mail.mail` queue parity with expanded `mail.mail`/`mail.notification`/`ir.mail_server` fields, due outgoing selection, sent/exception notification sync, retry/cancel/send/process call_kw dispatch, runtime named action aliases, and Odoo-shaped mail queue cron data. not recorded line 248
#160 added bounded persistent mail delivery parity for SMTP server resolution and MIME payloads: queue rows now carry From/Reply-To/custom headers/message attachments, nil-sender queue processing resolves explicit/default `ir.mail_server` rows with from-filter fallback, SMTP sends multipart attachments, failure types classify SMTP and invalid-recipient errors, and server-action mail hooks persist queue fields from templates/request values. not recorded line 249
#161 added Odoo 19 mail-state server-action warning parity for negative activity deadline ranges, template/model mismatch, transient target models, mail-thread/activity model requirements, and invalid generic follower/activity-user relation chains. Added persisted `ir.model` flags (`abstract`, `transient`, `is_mail_thread`, `is_mail_activity`, `is_mail_blacklist`), metadata-loader derivation, `res.partner` mail metadata, template/model lookup, relation-chain warning tests, and schema/migration coverage. not recorded line 250
#162 timed automation cursor now commits only after successful action execution; failed timed actions keep the previous window for retry. not recorded line 251
#163 expanded security record-rule evaluation with nested fields, optional equality, comparisons, string operators, x2many membership, and `child_of`/`parent_of` fallback semantics. not recorded line 252
#164 tightened record-rule `child_of`/`parent_of` semantics for persisted company hierarchies. Security rule evaluation now uses loaded `res.company.parent_id` trees for company IDs while retaining direct-membership fallback for generic domain evaluation. not recorded line 253

#206 added bounded safe-eval support for numeric division, `DateTime.now/today`, `datetime.now/today`, `datetime(...)`, `timedelta`, `relativedelta`, `.date()`, and Python-style `.strftime()`. not recorded line 296
#207 added bounded XML safe-eval `obj()` support for `obj().env.ref(...)`, `obj().env['model'].browse/search(...)`, `obj().browse/search(...)`, `.id`, `.ids`, relation-field traversal, `_get_id`, and search `limit`/`offset`/`order` keyword args. not recorded line 297
#208 added bounded XML safe-eval list comprehension support for `[expr for name in iterable]`, including local variable `.id`/`.ids` access and source-like x2many command generation. not recorded line 298
#209 added bounded top-level XML safe-eval `and/or` fallback plus `+` composition for strings, lists, and numbers, covering source-like optional value fallback and x2many list concatenation. not recorded line 299

#219 added asset registry directive parsing, recursive include expansion, include errors, path-based remove fallback, and runtime `ir.asset` application with Odoo sequence split (`<16`, manifests, `>=16`). not recorded line 312
#220 expanded OWL compatibility exports with `OwlError`, `useComponent`, `onWillDestroy`, `onError`, `markRaw`, `toRaw`, `whenReady`, `htmlEscape`, `batched`, `validate`, `validateType`, `loadFile`, `blockDom`, dependency-aware `useEffect`, and mounted external listeners. not recorded line 313

#224 added persisted advanced workflow-process lifecycle storage keyed by `(model, record_id)`, record-bound start/apply/delete wrappers, persisted workflow transition logs into `approval.log`, and aligned local workflow-process runtime metadata fields with migration DDL. not recorded line 319
#225 added persisted advanced workflow escalation date computation, due-process scanning, record-bound escalation application, and escalation approval-log persistence. not recorded line 320
#226 added ordered `approval.config` runtime progression for approve actions, condition-based next-state resolution, auto-approval advancement, and matching migration coverage for progression fields. not recorded line 321
#227 expanded OI workflow automation trigger bus to source triggers, including submit/approval/enter/final approve, reject, draft, forward, create, state-updated, committee approval, and backward-compatible state-change dispatch. not recorded line 322
#228 added Env-backed OI base approval dispatch for `approval_action_button`, `action_approve_all`, approval process/state-update wizards, HTTP `/web/dataset/call_button`, source `action` server-action buttons, and persisted approval logs/forwards/votes/cancellations. not recorded line 323
#229 added advanced `approval_transition_button` dispatch backed by persisted `workflow.process`, source-shaped workflow/node/transition/action loader, transition approval-log persistence, business record state/node write-back, and server-action node hooks. not recorded line 324
#230 added portable frontend RPC/dataset/action/session services, OI workflow action executors for approval buttons, transitions, approve-all, status/log windows, login-as session normalization, and source-compatible OI static assets for form/list actions, statusbar workflow states, approval-user popover, advanced workflow view routing, systray, and debug route behavior. not recorded line 325
#231 aligned frontend registry with Odoo-style category subregistries, sequence ordering, `force`, duplicate/missing-key errors, `contains`, `getAll`, `getEntries`, `remove`, validation hook, and `UPDATE` events while preserving existing `registries.*` aliases. not recorded line 326
#232 added Odoo-style `makeEnv` and `startServices` with env bus/readiness, dependency-ordered service startup, late registry `UPDATE` service starts, async metadata, missing-dependency errors, and default `rpc`, `dataset`, `action`, and `session` service registration for `useService` consumers. not recorded line 327
#233 added OWL `useService` async metadata protection for function services and object methods, including destroyed-component rejection/unresolved-success behavior and test patch hook parity. not recorded line 328
#234 added frontend ORM service parity surface with `x2ManyCommands`, `UPDATE_METHODS`, silent/cache clones, context-merged `call`, CRUD/search/web-read/web-save helpers, default `orm` service registration, and async metadata for `useService("orm")`. not recorded line 329
#235 expanded frontend e2e coverage beyond shell mount to exercise started webclient services, OI approval button dispatch through `/web/dataset/call_button`, action soft-reload handling, ORM `web_read`, session loading, and login-as context normalization. not recorded line 330

#237 aligned OI delegation runtime guards with source behavior: submit now rejects past start dates, overlap checks only consider confirmed delegations, and manual revoke rejects delegations whose end date is already in the past. not recorded line 332
#238 aligned accounting post/cancel safeguards with Odoo 19 behavior for locked accounting-date shifting, non-mutating secure-sequence gap failures, empty-move rejection, hashed cancel blocking, full lock checks on reset/cancel/unlink, and reversal-safe policy separation. not recorded line 333

#319 added OI workflow advance auto-start on record create: runtime registers a generic after-create hook, dispatcher selects ordered active `on_create` workflows, starts and persists one process per business record, writes business record workflow state, skips no-match create silently, and exposes `on_create` in workflow views. not recorded line 435

#321 added OI workflow advance email-transition parity: comment transitions reopen `workflow.process.wizard`, email transitions return source-shaped `mail.compose.message` actions without mutating workflow state, compose send resumes the transition with `MailComposed=true`, and HTTP tests cover call-button compose plus post-send completion. not recorded line 437
#322 added loaded `mail.compose.message` compose wizard parity for OI email transitions: base now seeds `mail.email_compose_message_wizard_form` and `mail.action_email_compose_message_wizard`, the workflow dispatcher falls back to the source XML ID when no custom wizard view is configured, and runtime/HTTP tests verify action view resolution plus view registry exposure. not recorded line 438
#323 added Odoo/OI `approval_auto_submit` create-context parity for classic workflow records: generic create/web-save propagate a sanitized `approval_auto_submit` context key, the after-create workflow hook performs source-shaped direct `_action_approve` behavior without requiring a clicked button, `on_submit`/`on_approve` automations fire, and HTTP/direct create tests verify context-gated behavior. not recorded line 439
#324 added OI workflow advance `workflow.create_context` action payload parity: runtime action hydration now emits source-style `multi_workflow_view` JSON for active workflows with form views, parses JSON/simple Python dict create contexts, honors action `multi_workflow_view=False`, exposes the payload through `/web/action/load`, and tests create/web-save propagation into `approval_auto_submit`. not recorded line 440
#325 added OI workflow advance `workflow_view_id` open-record parity: advanced workflow write-back persists the workflow form view ID onto business records when the model exposes it, extension metadata asserts the source field, generic `web_read` now returns many2one fields as `[id, display_name]`, and HTTP/direct tests cover tuple-shaped `workflow_view_id`. not recorded line 441
#326 added OI workflow advance computed pre-process `workflow_view_id` parity. Generic HTTP `web_read`, `web_search_read`, `web_save`, `web_save_multi`, and `web_name_search` now compute non-persisted `workflow_view_id` tuples for records with no workflow node by selecting the first active matching workflow by sequence/id and condition, while node records read their workflow view directly. Focused workflow and HTTP tests passed. focused tests passed line 442
#327 added OI workflow advance auto email-transition compose handling. Email transitions reached from automatic nodes now stop before advancing, return the compose wizard action, persist the current auto-node workflow state plus pending transition ID, and complete the transition only after compose send with `mail_compose` semantics. Focused workflow and HTTP email-transition tests passed. focused tests passed line 443
#328 source-audited OI workflow `create_context` safe_eval behavior and tightened local literal parsing for action `multi_workflow_view` payloads. OI passes no explicit `env`, `user`, `context`, or `uid` locals for workflow `create_context`; local parsing now handles source-shaped Python dict literals with booleans, `None`, strings containing commas, lists, and tuples. Focused workflow/runtime tests passed. focused tests passed line 444
#329 added frontend workflow-selection coverage. Focused frontend tests passed. focused tests passed line 445
#330 added invalid OI workflow `create_context` propagation parity for action hydration. #331 added bounded OI `workflow.process.wizard` computed default/domain parity. `default_get` now hydrates model, record, record name, process, workflow, node, allowed transition IDs, selected transition, and required-comment state from the current workflow process; the wizard form now carries the transition domain and object footer metadata. #332 added registry-time abstract `_inherit` field propagation for source-style `approval.record` consumers. Concrete models such as `delegation` now receive base approval, mail thread/activity, and advanced workflow fields from inherited abstract models; `approval_state_id` now targets `approval.config` instead of `approval.settings.state`. #333 added OI `approval.automation.code` runtime handling and schema parity with source-compatible ordering: automations now execute deterministically by `sequence,id`, filter misses skip only the current automation, non-empty Python code fails closed before side effects unless a bounded code runner is installed, source-like active context metadata is passed to code/server actions, server actions run before mail templates, and regression coverage checks disabled code, ordering, filter skip, active context, migration schema, and full CI. not recorded line 450

#341 wired bootstrapped OI delegation runtime into the app security engine. `BootstrapOI` now creates a configured delegation service from persisted `res.groups` metadata, binds it as the security delegation provider, exposes it on `runtime.App`, and regression coverage proves confirmed delegations affect effective groups without manual binding. not recorded line 466

#345 added a clean-room Odoo-like arch-to-read-spec builder for frontend list/form loading: field nodes are parsed from XML, many2one/reference fields request `display_name`, x2many fields build nested related-model specs with context/limit/order, read contexts include `bin_size`, and focused tests cover relational list `web_search_read` specs plus form `res_id` `web_read`. focused tests passed line 472

#347 expanded frontend Odoo-style Python expression evaluation beyond literals with boolean, comparison, membership, arithmetic, ternary, attribute access, indexing, grouped expressions, string helpers, safe builtins, bounded date helpers, `relativedelta`, and dynamic `invisible`/`column_invisible` filtering in read specs and rendered list/form field names. not recorded line 476

#360 source-audit correction applied for Odoo 19 fiscal tax maps: one source tax can map to multiple destination taxes. `FiscalPosition` now supports source-to-many tax mappings, record fiscal-position loading builds deterministic multi-destination maps from `account.tax.original_tax_ids`, and regression tests cover multi-tax mapping through both pure helpers and move-line normalization. not recorded line 505
#361 added account.move write propagation for fiscal-position-sensitive invoice lines. #362 added persisted `res.company` accounting lock-date fields plus `account.lock_exception` record normalization. #364 aligned `account.lock_exception` ACLs with Odoo source shape: base users read only, account managers read/create only, no direct write/unlink grant in accounting security fixtures. not recorded line 512
#365 added Odoo-compatible lock-removal exceptions and company soft-lock recreation. Explicit false/empty soft-lock fields now count as the selected exception field and clear only that soft lock; company soft-lock writes recreate active affected exceptions with refreshed `company_lock_date` and revoke originals. #366 `account.lock_exception/action_revoke` now resolves account-manager permission dynamically from `ir.model.data` XMLID `account.group_account_manager`, with `complete_name` fallback and no broad display-name overgrant, instead of using a fixed HTTP-side group ID. Regression coverage creates the XMLID row and uses its actual group ID. not recorded line 514
#367 company soft-lock writes now snapshot and restore the full `account.lock_exception` store, including records and `nextID`, when exception recreation fails after partial side effects. Regression coverage forces one replacement create, fails the second, and verifies company rows, originals, replacements, and ID sequencing are restored. not recorded line 515
#368 `account.move.line` write lock checks now follow Odoo's protected-field split: nonprotected posted-line fields do not trigger lock errors, fiscal-protected fields check fiscal/sale/purchase/hard locks only, tax-protected fields check tax/hard locks only when the edited line affects tax reports, and posted tax field edits are refused directly. Regression coverage includes nonprotected writes, fiscal-protected writes, untaxed-line balance writes, taxed-line balance writes, and posted `tax_ids` edits. not recorded line 516
#369 `account.move.line` create/write/unlink now posts Odoo-style parent `account.move` chatter messages for posted-once moves unless `tracking_disable` is set. #370 generic `mail.thread.message_post` now accepts tracking values through `PostRequest`, `/web/dataset/call_kw/*/message_post`, and `/mail/message/post`. #371 `/mail/thread/messages` now includes raw `mail.message.tracking_value_ids`, `data["mail.tracking.value"]`, and per-message Odoo-style formatted `trackingValues` with `fieldInfo`, typed `oldValue`, and typed `newValue`. #372 direct `mail.message.create` now processes `tracking_value_ids` command payloads through the record layer. Create commands `[0, 0, values]` force `mail_message_id` to the new message, link parent `tracking_value_ids` to created IDs, support bounded link/update/set commands, and restore `mail.message` plus `mail.tracking.value` stores and ID counters on tracking failure. Added record and HTTP call_kw regression coverage. not recorded line 523
#373 added Odoo-compatible `/mail/message/update_content` handling. Strict message content updates now reject messages with tracking values, reject non-comment messages, update comment bodies with the edited marker, and support partner/attachment replacement through the mail service and HTTP route. Source audit corrected that raw `mail.message.write` remains allowed, so direct write stays writable while the strict route enforces the Odoo UI/content-update rule. Added mail, record, and HTTP regression coverage. not recorded line 525
#374 `/mail/message/update_content` now enforces edit authorization before content mutation. Superuser ID 1, users in `base.group_system`, and users whose `res.users.partner_id` matches `mail.message.author_id` can edit; other users receive forbidden responses. Added missing standard `res.users.partner_id` base field, seed data, and migration coverage. Focused mail, HTTP, base, and migration tests passed. focused tests passed line 527
#375 updated `/mail/message/update_content` against Odoo 19 source audit: admin edit override now keys on `base.group_erp_manager` with implied-group policy support, update reads/writes use system context only after explicit access authorization, authors can edit through the strict route even when raw `mail.message.write` is ACL-denied, and `body:null` is treated as no body update. Added policy-backed author/admin tests and route regression for body-null preservation. not recorded line 529
#376 `/mail/message/update_content` now accepts `attachment_tokens`, validates attachment ownership, links pending `mail.compose.message` / `mail.scheduled.message` attachments onto the target thread, appends new attachment links without dropping existing links, and deletes current attachments on explicit empty `attachment_ids`. Added mail service and HTTP route regression coverage. not recorded line 531
#377 attachment ownership now uses Odoo-style scoped limited-field HMAC tokens with `database.secret` for `attachment_ownership` instead of stored `ir.attachment.access_token`; explicit empty `attachment_tokens` is treated as no token, and count errors only fire when a non-empty token list length mismatches the attachment payload. Added regressions rejecting stored access tokens and accepting generated scoped ownership tokens. regression coverage added line 533
#378 added guest author ownership foundations: `mail.message.author_guest_id`, expanded `mail.guest`, `discuss.channel.member.guest_id`, and `mail.presence.guest_id` schema/migrations; `/mail/message/update_content` parses valid `dgid=<guest_id>|<access_token>` cookies into context; guest-authored messages can be edited by their matching guest and reject bad guest cookies. Added mail service, HTTP route, base model, and migration tests. not recorded line 534
#379 added Odoo discuss-channel `/mail/message/update_content` override parity. #380 `/mail/message/post` and dataset `message_post` now propagate `attachment_tokens`, distinguish absent vs explicit `attachment_ids`, validate Odoo-style scoped attachment ownership before posting, reject stored `ir.attachment.access_token` as an ownership substitute, reject non-empty token count mismatches, and relink accepted pending compose/scheduled attachments to the posted thread.
