#387 advanced workflow approval enrichment now adds active delegated approvers from the delegation service, using source field-order department resolution (`employee_id`, `x_employee_id`, `department_id`, `x_department_id`) and preserving the source fallback where empty record departments do not restrict delegation lines. #388 tightened advanced delegated approver parity against OI source: delegation users now require the active workflow approval group, exclude users already in the approver set, and use `hr.department.parent_id` descendant expansion so delegation lines whose departments are `child_of` the record department apply. #390 added installable Odoo 19 HR base metadata for `hr.department`, `hr.employee`, `hr.job`, `hr.employee.category`, and HR `res.users` links, wired HR into OI runtime manifests/model registration/metadata/env composition before delegation extensions, and added persistent HR schema plus focused addon/runtime/migration coverage. #391 advanced workflow approval enrichment now filters computed approvers by target-record `read` access after direct/group/delegated approvers are combined, using per-candidate record contexts so ACLs and record rules exclude users who cannot read the approval record. Partner approvers are recomputed from the filtered user set. Added regression coverage for group approvers where one candidate is denied by a record rule. not recorded line 555
#392 committee done-user parity now covers classic and advanced workflow paths. Classic committee approvals hold on same state until remaining users are exhausted or the committee limit is reached, exclude done approvers from visible approval buttons, and clear committee done users on completion. Advanced workflow nodes now hydrate `responsible_committee` metadata, reject repeat approvals by done users, subtract `approval_done_user_ids` from computed `approval_user_ids`, recompute approval partners from current approvers, and clear done users on node entry. Added focused classic, advanced in-memory, and dispatch persistence regressions. Full CI passed. full CI passed line 557
#393 active forward override parity now covers classic and advanced workflow paths. Classic forward actions replace effective approvers, persist `approval_state_id`, recompute partner/can-approve mirrors, allow forwarded users to approve without original approval groups, and deactivate active forwards on state changes. Advanced workflow enrichment now replaces node responsible/delegated approvers with active `approval.forward` users matched by `workflow_node_id`, persists workflow-node forward metadata, recomputes process/record mirrors, and deactivates forwards on node completion. Added schema/migration support for `approval_forward.workflow_node_id`, plus classic/advanced regressions. Full CI passed. full CI passed line 559
#394 classic workflow approval hydration now expands current approval-config users/groups, appends active delegated approvers when no active forward exists, filters approvers by record read access, preserves committee done-user exclusion, and carries active delegation attribution into classic approval logs. Advanced approval logs now also persist `delegation_id` for delegated manual transitions. Added delegation-service, classic approval dispatch, and advanced transition regressions. Focused workflow/delegation/runtime/OI tests passed. focused tests passed line 561
#395 added bounded OI 18-style dynamic approver evaluation for classic `approval.config.user_python_code` and advanced `workflow.node.responsible_python_code`. Runtime hydration now loads both fields, evaluates `result = ...` against the record/env safe-eval surface, adds results to direct configured users, skips group users when code is present, fails closed on unsupported code, and prevents advanced group fallback from authorizing users excluded by computed approver IDs. Added `workflow_node.responsible_python_code` metadata/schema/migration coverage plus classic and advanced dispatch regressions. Focused data/workflow/migration tests passed. focused tests passed line 563
#396 workflow delegation department resolution now uses a system-context environment for approval-record department lookup and `hr.department` descendant expansion while preserving candidate read-access filtering under each approver's own context. Added restrictive-HR regressions for classic approval hydration and advanced auto-start delegation where users can read the target record but cannot read HR records. Focused workflow/data/migration/runtime/OI tests passed. focused tests passed line 565
#397 expanded XML safe-eval toward Odoo 19 source fixture coverage with `obj(ref(...))` recordsets, bounded string methods (`replace`, `lower`, `upper`, `split`), list/tuple/string indexing including negative indexes, `datetime(...).weekday()`, and `timedelta(days=...)` integer expressions using source-like `datetime.today().weekday() +/- N`. Added loader regressions for Odoo 19 enterprise payroll/demo string expressions and planning demo weekday/timedelta expressions. Focused data tests passed. focused tests passed line 567
#398 added workflow-bound approval activity synchronization for classic approval states and advanced workflow nodes. The runtime now loads classic `schedule_activity`/`schedule_activity_days`, honors advanced `schedule_activity_enabled`, creates one automated `Approval` activity per current approver with `Waiting Approval` summary and computed deadline, prunes obsolete/duplicate approval activities on approval/user changes, and clears the business record deadline when leaving schedulable approval states. Added classic and advanced dispatch regressions. Focused workflow/mail/OI tests passed. focused tests passed line 570
#399 matched OI approval activity deadline-field semantics. Classic approval configs and advanced workflow nodes now resolve `schedule_activity_field_id` through `ir.model.fields`; activity deadlines use that record date/datetime value when present, fall back to the state-update date when absent, then add `schedule_activity_days`. Added `workflow_node.schedule_activity_field_id` schema/migration coverage and classic/advanced dispatch regressions. regression coverage added line 572
#400 added OI `mail.activity.hide_in_chatter` parity for automated approval activities. Base mail activity metadata/schema now includes `hide_in_chatter`; record normalization computes it from `oi_workflow.activity_type_approval` plus `automated`, including recompute when automation status changes. Workflow-created approval activities now read as hidden. Added mail activity and workflow regressions plus migration coverage. regression coverage added line 574
#401 wired runtime workflow mailer support so workflow automation `template_ids` and email buttons can send real `mail.template` batches. Runtime dispatchers now receive `envActionHooks` as `workflow.Mailer`; the hook delegates to `mail.SendTemplateBatch`, producing rendered `mail.message`, `mail.mail`, and scheduled-message rows. Added runtime regression coverage for rendered approval-template email creation. not recorded line 576
#402 added source-shaped delegation email CC expansion for workflow template sends. `mail.SendTemplateBatch` now loads `mail.template.delegation_group_ids` and accepts a CC expansion hook; runtime workflow mail finds recipient employees from rendered To/CC or partner recipients, locates active confirmed delegations, filters delegation lines by the template delegation groups, and appends delegate employee work emails to `mail.mail.email_cc` without duplicates. Added runtime regression coverage for delegated CC injection. not recorded line 578
#403 wired advanced workflow escalation into the scheduled-action path. `workflow.process.escalation` is now a registered runtime Go action, scheduler cron execution carries `user_id`, due `workflow.process` rows are escalated through the dispatcher, and process state, business-record mirrors, approval logs, approval activities, forwards, and action metadata are persisted after escalation. Added dispatcher, scheduler, and runtime regressions. regression coverage added line 580
#404 completed workflow email compose continuation parity for classic and advanced paths. Classic `approval.buttons` now load/persist `email_wizard_form_id` and `email_next_action`, email buttons open `mail.compose.message` with `approval_button_id`, required-comment buttons route through `approval.process.wizard` before compose, compose send resumes `email_next_action` without re-sending mail, and HTTP returns the resumed workflow action. Advanced compose completion now preserves delegation attribution, deactivates forwards after transition, and returns resumed actions. Added workflow, HTTP, migration, and OI metadata regressions. regression coverage added line 582
#405 closed bounded flowchart/list routing parity. The advanced workflow asset now provides the source-shaped `oi_workflow_advance.confirm_view` dialog, workflow forms mark `flowchart` with `widget="flowchart"`, workflow flowchart values are emitted as flowchart.js DSL, the frontend flowchart package can parse that DSL, and HTTP `get_views` injects hidden `workflow_view_id` into list/kanban views for advanced workflow-enabled models so list open routing can use workflow-specific form views. Added workflow, HTTP, frontend, and addon regressions. regression coverage added line 584
#406 expanded XML safe-eval for Odoo 19 fixture parity with bounded `str`/`dict`/`list` builtins, `dict(..., **{...})`, nested call-argument expressions, Python-style list/tuple/dict repr, `%s`/`%d`/`%i` string formatting, datetime `.year`/`.month`/`.day`, `DateTime(...)` named constructor arguments, top-level subtraction, floor division, `obj().env.company`, `obj().env.user` field traversal plus `has_group`, no-op `sudo`/`with_context`, recordset `mapped`, bounded `filtered(lambda ...)`, simple slicing, recordset `create`, `fields_get`, and `default_get`. Added loader regressions using Odoo 19 source-shaped account budget, gamification, rental, helpdesk-stock, purchase quality, and POS loyalty expressions. regression coverage added line 586
#407 added bounded XML f-string eval support for source-shaped `f'Budget {datetime.now().year}: Home Construction'` and `f'{datetime.now().year + 1}-01-01'` payloads from Odoo 19 project accounting budget demo data. Focused data tests passed. focused tests passed line 588
#408 added bounded source-safe-eval support for Odoo 19 XML multi-generator list comprehensions, `range(...)`, `map(str, ...)`, string literal method chaining, `','.join([...])`, and `'...{}'.format(...)`. Added regressions covering Google Reserve slot-style generated x2many commands, embedded-action comma ordering, and localized tax XML-ID formatting. regression coverage added line 590
#409 added bounded Odoo `__manifest__.py` parser support for Python dict manifests, module-name technical-name override, Python string/list/bool values, source license/version capture, auto-install dependency lists, manifest asset tuple operations (`include`, `remove`, `before`, `after`, `replace`), and runtime application of parsed asset operations. Added module parser and runtime asset-operation regressions. regression coverage added line 592
#410 loaded Odoo 19 `base/security/base_groups.xml` into the base manifest with bootstrap-only predeclared groups for forward/user seed references, added source group fields (`privilege_id`, `implied_by_ids`, `user_ids`, `comment`, `api_key_duration`) plus SQL/migration coverage, synchronized `implied_by_ids` into effective `implied_ids` and group `user_ids` into user group aliases, and added XML scalar coercion for plain-text bool/int/float fields used by source fixtures. Focused data/base/record/migrate tests passed. focused tests passed line 594
#411 replaced local reduced `base_security.xml` with Odoo 19 source `base/security/base_security.xml`, added missing base security anchor models/tables (`res.users.log`, `ir.ui.view.custom`, `res.currency.rate`, `change.password.*`, `res.users.identitycheck`, `res.device*`, `properties.base.definition`), added source rule fields on `res.users`/`res.partner`, fixed `ir.model.data` schema drift for `complete_name`/`noupdate`, and extended persisted rule parsing for source domain identifiers (`user.id`, `user.ids`, `user.commercial_partner_id.id`, `company_ids`, `company_ids + [False]`). Focused base/security/migrate tests passed and source rules hydrate into the security engine. focused tests passed line 596
#412 added stored Odoo 19 semantics for base security rule fields. `res.partner.parent_id`, `commercial_partner_id`, and `partner_share` now recompute on partner/user/group changes; `res.users.share` derives from effective `base.group_user` closure and `commercial_partner_id` delegates through the linked partner; group `user_ids`/implication writes recompute users after source group data loads. Persisted security now loads user commercial partners and partner hierarchy, and record rules support `res.partner` `id child_of user.commercial_partner_id.id` while preserving company hierarchy `child_of`/`parent_of`. Added focused base, record, security, migration, and source-domain rule-effect tests for partner sharing, users sharing, and portal commercial-partner narrowing. `go test ./...` passed. not recorded line 598
#413 replaced the remaining reduced base ACL matrix with Odoo 19 source `base/security/ir.model.access.csv`, added the source ACL anchor models/tables (`decimal.precision`, `ir.exports*`, `ir.model.*` metadata helpers, module dependency/exclusion, user API key wizards, partner categories, language/module/merge wizards, profiling/demo/report/config helpers, `ir.mail_server`, `ir.logging`, `ir.profile`, and related transients), and asserted source ACL IDs/counts in base data loading. Added loaded-source security flow coverage that pairs `Engine.Check` ACL decisions with `CheckRecord` rule decisions for active internal, portal, and public-style users across `res.partner`, `res.users`, and `res.company`. Focused base, migrate, and security tests passed. focused tests passed line 600
#414 loaded persisted `ir.model.fields.groups` into the security engine, mapped source group XML IDs to runtime group IDs, preserved explicit no-access field restrictions, and marked Odoo 19 source-restricted base fields (`ir.attachment.access_token`, `ir.actions.server.code`, SMTP credential/certificate fields, and `res.users.identitycheck.request`). Added schema/migration coverage and loaded-source field filtering assertions for system, portal, public, and superuser contexts. not recorded line 602
#415 aligned base user/partner active-share semantics with Odoo 19 source behavior. Inactive linked users no longer force `res.partner.partner_share=false`; `res.users` create syncs linked partner active state; user reactivation unarchives the linked partner; archiving a partner linked to an active user is rejected; `res.users.active_partner` metadata/schema is present. `fields_get` now applies field-group filtering through the record policy, with loaded-source no-access coverage for `res.users.identitycheck.request`. not recorded line 604
#416 expanded `ir.module.category` toward the Odoo 19 source model with child/module/privilege relations, `visible`, `exclusive`, and `xml_id` metadata, in-memory default parity for visible/exclusive, source fixture assertions, and migration/bootstrap SQL coverage. not recorded line 606
#417 added bounded Odoo 19 view group pruning. Persisted `ir.ui.view` loading now hydrates `inherit_id`, `priority`, `active`, and `groups_id`; inactive views are skipped; explicit restricted view IDs are rejected for users outside view `groups_id`; `/web/view/load` and `get_views` use effective groups; XML node `groups` expressions support comma OR and `!` negatives through `ir.model.data`, strip surviving `groups` attributes, drop denied subtrees, and lift allowed empty `<t groups>` wrappers. Added registry, runtime, and HTTP regressions. regression coverage added line 608
#418 expanded `res.groups`/`res.groups.privilege` toward Odoo 19 hierarchy payload parity. Base metadata and SQL now expose source-facing group fields including `full_name`, `share`, `sequence`, transitive implied/implied-by sets, disjoint user-type groups, all users/count, related model/menu/view/rule access fields, and `view_group_hierarchy`; privileges now expose `description`, `placeholder`, and `group_ids`. Record normalization now provides source defaults and validates group name/API-key duration constraints, while derived mirrors recompute from persisted groups, users, XML IDs, categories, and privileges. Added base, migration, and record regressions. regression coverage added line 610
#419 expanded `res.users` group payload parity for Odoo 19. Base metadata and SQL now expose `all_group_ids`, `accesses_count`, `rules_count`, `groups_count`, `view_group_hierarchy`, and `role`; user derived fields compute effective group closure, access/rule counts, role, share, commercial partner, and widget hierarchy on create/write/read. Backend `group_ids`/legacy `groups_id` writes now decode Odoo x2many commands `3`, `4`, `5`, and `6`, mirror both explicit group fields, and base seed users now use source-facing `group_ids`. Added base, migration, record, and HTTP `web_save` regressions. regression coverage added line 612
#420 closed the bounded `res_user_group_ids` widget pass. Frontend form rendering now recognizes `widget="res_user_group_ids"`, requests `all_group_ids`, `view_group_hierarchy`, and `role` dependencies, renders backend-shaped group hierarchy sections, and emits Odoo-shaped x2many set command lists. Backend now rejects users whose effective group closure spans multiple exclusive user-type groups, sorts hierarchy privilege groups by Odoo implied-depth/sequence/id order, sorts category privileges by sequence, and implements bounded `res.users.role` onchange group rewrites. Added frontend, record, and HTTP regressions. regression coverage added line 614
#421 improved the `res_user_group_ids` widget and group helper API. Privileged access groups now render as one select control per privilege with the source `placeholder`, choosing a value replaces other explicit groups from that privilege, non-privileged extra rights remain checkboxes, and group debug metadata is exposed through titles/data attributes for implied, implied-by, and disjoint groups. HTTP now handles `res.groups._get_group_definitions` with external refs/supersets/disjoints and `res.groups.action_show_all_users` with the Odoo-shaped users action. Added frontend and HTTP regressions. regression coverage added line 616
#422 implemented dynamic `ir.ui.view` inheritance composition. Registry now resolves combined arches for primary and extension views, applies recursive extension specs by priority/id, respects primary inheritance boundaries, supports Odoo-style `<xpath>` and direct locator positions (`inside`, `before`, `after`, `replace`, `attributes`), returns strict missing-locator errors, hydrates `mode`/`primary` from runtime records, and serves composed arches from `get_views` and `/web/view/load`. Added registry, runtime, and HTTP regressions. regression coverage added line 617
#423 added bounded Odoo `position="move"` inheritance support. Direct child move specs now extract the first matching existing XML node, remove it from the original parent, and insert it through `inside`, `before`, `after`, or `replace` content handling. Missing move locators and move specs with child content now fail strictly. Added move relocation and invalid-spec regressions. regression coverage added line 618
#424 expanded view inheritance parity for `replace mode="inner"` and XPath locator coverage. Replace-inner now keeps the target element and attributes while replacing only child content, supports direct-child move specs inside the replacement body, rejects unsupported replace modes, fixes absolute XPath handling, and supports bounded positional predicates such as `//field[2]`. Added replace-inner, indexed XPath, invalid-mode, and absolute-path regressions. regression coverage added line 619
#425 expanded `position="attributes"` inheritance parity. Attribute specs now support Odoo-style `add`, `remove`, and `separator`, empty attribute deletion, Python-expression attribute mutation for `readonly`/`required`/`invisible`/`column_invisible`/`t-if`/`t-elif`/`decoration-*`, strict errors for invalid `<attribute>` keys, text with add/remove, and invalid expression separators. Added class/group/expression/error regressions. regression coverage added line 620
#426 expanded XPath locator parity for Odoo `hasclass()` predicates. View inheritance now matches class names as exact whitespace-delimited tokens, supports single-quoted and double-quoted args, multi-arg `hasclass('a', 'b')`, chained `and` forms, combinations with attribute predicates such as `[@name='x'][hasclass('btn-primary')]`, and `not(hasclass('x'))` exclusions used by source views. Added exact-token, partial-token rejection, attribute-combined, multi-arg, `and`, and `not` regressions. regression coverage added line 621
#427 expanded XPath path traversal parity for interior descendant axes. Locator parsing now preserves `/` versus `//` separators while respecting quotes and bracket predicates, applies `A//B` as a depth-first descendant search below each matched `A`, supports absolute interior forms such as `/form/sheet//field[@name='target']`, repeated mixed path traversal, and retains direct-child `/` behavior. Added source-shaped regressions for OI workflow footer state fields, absolute interior descendant paths, and direct-child slash preservation. regression coverage added line 622
#428 expanded XPath positional predicate parity. Step matching now treats `[N]` and `[last()]` as 1-based, sibling-local filters after the current step's tag, attribute, `hasclass()`, and `not(hasclass())` predicates, matching Odoo/lxml semantics for non-parenthesized paths. Added regressions for class-filtered second candidate selection, per-parent `last()` behavior, and source-shaped interior descendant paths such as `//table[@name='invoice_line_table']/tbody//tr[1]//td[1]`. regression coverage added line 623
#429 expanded XPath parent-step parity. Path traversal now supports full-segment `..` after child, descendant, absolute, `.//`, and relative selectors, repeated parent climbs, continuing from the climbed node, root-parent no-match behavior, and node-set deduplication in document order before Odoo first-match mutation. Added regressions for source-shaped parent-cell selection (`//span[@t-field='line.price_unit']/..`), OI-style climb-then-child selection (`//field[@name='parent_id']/../../group[2]`), and deduped parent node-set ordering. regression coverage added line 624
#430 expanded bounded XPath `contains()` predicate parity. Step predicates now support raw case-sensitive `contains(@attr, 'literal')`, direct-text `contains(text(), 'literal')`, and the Odoo class-token idiom `contains(concat(' ', normalize-space(@class), ' '), ' token ')`; unsupported `contains()` forms now fail closed instead of degenerating into tag-only matches. Added regressions for cart total attr contains with descendant `last()`, text contains plus parent climb, missing-substring rejection, and the modal-title class-token idiom. regression coverage added line 625
#431 expanded bounded XPath boolean `or` parity. Step predicates now support top-level whitespace-delimited `or` over attribute equality operands such as `@name='journal_line_ids' or @name='line_ids'`, combine OR groups with other step filters through AND semantics, preserve `or` inside quoted attribute values as literals, and fail closed for unsupported mixed forms such as `@name='x' or contains(...)`. Added regressions for account move line parent alternatives, address alternative parent traversal, complex-OR false-positive prevention, and exact QWeb `@t-if` values containing `or`. regression coverage added line 626
#432 expanded bounded XPath union and grouped ordinal parity. Locator evaluation now splits top-level `|` outside quotes, predicates, and parentheses, evaluates branches independently, dedupes and merges by document order, and supports parenthesized global node-set ordinals `(expr)[N]` and `(expr)[last()]` with optional suffix traversal relative to the selected node. Added regressions for quoted-`or` grouped ordinals, global `last()` versus per-parent step `last()`, document-order first match for unions, and grouped ordinal suffix paths used by website card templates. regression coverage added line 627
#433 expanded bounded XPath explicit-axis parity. Path traversal now supports source-used `following-sibling::`, `parent::`, and `ancestor::` axes with existing bounded node tests/predicates, axis-local numeric/`last()` filtering for following siblings, suffix traversal relative to matched axis nodes, and fail-closed behavior for unsupported axes. Added regressions for first following sibling replacement, ancestor-to-first-child traversal, parent-axis mutation, and unsupported axis no-match. regression coverage added line 628
#434 expanded source-proven XPath function predicate parity for view/template inheritance. Step predicates now support `[node()]` child-node existence checks used by Odoo 19 web templates and `*[local-name()='...']` wildcard local-name equality used by enterprise JPK export templates, preventing unsupported predicates from degrading into broad tag-only matches for these source shapes. Added regressions for non-empty template child selection and wildcard local-name replacement scoping. regression coverage added line 629
#435 added source-used current-node XPath support. `expr="."` now selects the current inherited root so root-level template inheritance can append inside or replace the root, covering source shapes found in payment, enterprise accountant/web templates, and OI reports. Added regressions for root inside insertion and root replacement. regression coverage added line 630
#436 expanded source-used XPath attribute predicate parity. Step predicates now support `[@attr]` attribute presence, `[not(@attr)]` absence checks, and `[not(@attr='value')]` exact-value exclusion, including combinations with existing `and` splitting and positional predicates. Added source-shaped regressions for hierarchy template `t-if` presence, unnamed group selection, and purchase currency not-invisible selection. regression coverage added line 631
#437 expanded source-used XPath relative child predicate parity. Nested predicate parsing now respects bracket depth, and step predicates can require relative child/path matches such as `div[table[@name='invoices']]`, `div[./field[@name='partner_ids']]`, nested child predicates such as `div[hasclass('row')][div[hasclass('col-6')][@t-if='o.memo']]`, child-attribute conditions like `field/@name = '...'`, and wildcard child checks with quoted attribute values. Added regressions covering standard, enterprise, website-builder, and report-shaped expressions. regression coverage added line 632
#438 added bounded QWeb/OWL template inheritance support to the frontend template compiler. The compiler now resolves `t-inherit` before render-function generation, supports extension templates that mutate parents without emitting anonymous templates, primary templates that clone/register derived names, XPath/direct patch locators, `inside`/`before`/`after`/`replace`/`attributes`, `$0` replacement preservation, attribute text set/delete and add/remove/separator token mutation, and exact-token multi-arg `hasclass(...)`. Added frontend regressions for source-shaped OI/web dialog and enterprise gantt inheritance patterns plus missing-parent errors. regression coverage added line 633
#439 added OI workflow state-write trigger parity. The record environment now supports transactional after-write hooks with rollback of hook side effects; runtime bootstrap registers the workflow dispatcher hook; and workflow-controlled state changes now run `on_state_updated` automations, append approval logs, persist `last_state_update`, deactivate active forwards, and suppress recursive hook writes. Added record rollback and workflow dispatch regressions for state-change writes and non-state writes. regression coverage added line 634
#440 added OI workflow unlink guard and approval-log cleanup parity. The record environment now supports transactional before-unlink hooks inherited through derived contexts; runtime bootstrap registers the workflow unlink hook; workflow-enabled records can only be deleted from draft/cancel-family states; denied unlinks preserve records and logs; allowed unlinks remove related `approval.log` rows before deleting the target. Added rollback coverage for unlink hook side effects and workflow regressions for approved-state denial plus draft cleanup. Remaining deferred gap: dynamic OI `_get_view` runtime mutation. regression coverage added line 635
#441 added bounded OI dynamic `_get_view` backend mutation parity. `get_views` and `/web/view/load` now apply workflow-driven form/list arch mutation before group pruning: form sheets receive/reuse button boxes with cancellation stat buttons, form headers receive hidden approval fields, approval action buttons, approval user info, and statusbar duration/dynamic visibility attrs, and list roots receive `show_action_approve_all` when settings/groups allow it. Added HTTP regressions for get_views and view/load mutation, OI default-true settings, allowed/denied group button pruning, statusbar attrs, button box injection, and approve-all list flags. regression coverage added line 636
#442 tightened OI view/frontend parity. Model metadata now carries source-compatible default form-field readonly expressions, and dynamic approval form mutation applies them while preserving OI skip rules for `state`, existing readonly attrs, `invisible="0"`/`False`, and readonly model fields. Generated approval buttons now preserve `data-hotkey`; studio `get_views` suppresses runtime button/statusbar/list mutations while still adding hidden approval fields; advanced list/kanban `workflow_view_id` injection now emits `invisible="1" column_invisible="1" readonly="1"`. The webclient list renderer now consumes `show_action_approve_all="true"` by rendering a selectable Approve toolbar action that confirms, calls local `action_approve_all` through `call_button`, runs returned actions, refreshes, and emits a workflow event. Added HTTP and frontend regressions. regression coverage added line 637
#443 added advanced OI workflow form button mutation. Approval settings now load `advance`; advanced forms inject hidden `workflow_transition_ids`/`workflow_node_id`, prepend one-button `approval_transition_wizard` nodes, prepend multi-transition `approval_transition_button` nodes, preserve context/icon/args/validate metadata, order buttons by workflow/node/transition sequence, and prefilter transition buttons by current groups or superuser. Added HTTP regression coverage for wizard buttons, multi buttons, restricted transition pruning, advanced hidden fields, and transition-before-classic ordering. not recorded line 638
#444 added bounded OI frontend approval button preflight parity. The webclient form renderer now emits object buttons from form XML, decodes XML entities in attributes, evaluates button invisibility against current values, confirms before dispatch, applies OI `beforeExecuteActionButton` semantics for `validate_form`, mutates `approved_button_clicked` from approval button args or explicit attrs, runs custom/native form validation, calls `/web/dataset/call_button` with `[active_ids, *args]`, forwards returned actions, refreshes, and emits a workflow button event. Added frontend regressions for approval action buttons, advanced transition buttons with `validate_form="False"`, entity-decoded context, hidden transition pruning, on-update mutation, validation, action forwarding, and refresh behavior. regression coverage added line 639
#445 added bounded OI workflow statusbar frontend parity. Form rendering now recognizes selection fields with `statusbar`, `statusbar_state_duration`, or `statusbar_visible`, applies OI `WORKFLOW` dynamic filtering from `workflow_states`, renders selected state metadata, formats per-state durations from `duration_state_tracking`, and includes the widget dependency in form read specifications. Added frontend regression coverage for visible workflow states, selected state marking, rejected-state pruning, and duration formatting. not recorded line 640
#446 added OI advanced transition wizard dispatch parity. The backend now handles generated `approval_transition_wizard` buttons, validates the current workflow node and available transitions, opens `workflow.process.wizard` with node-level `wizard_view_id`, active record context, and optional default transition, and uses the same node-level wizard view for comment-required transition buttons. Advanced workflow loading now preserves node `wizard_view_id`. Frontend button regression now covers one-button wizard dispatch and act-window forwarding; backend and HTTP regressions cover direct dispatcher and `/web/dataset/call_button` wizard actions. regression coverage added line 641
#447 added bounded OI approval user info popover parity. The webclient now renders the `approval_user_info` action button, fetches `approval_user_ids` and `approval_done_user_ids` through `webRead`, renders done and waiting approver rows with avatars, suppresses login-as for the current user, emits login-as links for system debug users, and emits a workflow popover event. Replaced local OI XML stubs for `ApprovalUserInfo` and `ViewButtonTooltip` with source-shaped templates. Added frontend and addon regressions. regression coverage added line 642
#448 added bounded OI update-status and approval-log action menu parity. Form/list rendering now exposes workflow action-menu buttons when source conditions hold (`state` field plus system user for Update Status, non-related `user_can_approve` for Approval Log), opens `approval.state.update` with source-shaped `default_res_model/default_res_ids`, opens `approval.log` with form/list-specific domains and context flags, disables list actions until selection, and wires close-refresh callbacks for status updates. Added frontend regressions for form and list action shapes, selection gating, log domains, and refresh callback wiring. regression coverage added line 643
#449 closed bounded OI approval button source-parity gaps. Classic approval buttons now render in source order (`sequence,id`), skip mismatched stored `model` values, serialize `groups` with existing XML IDs and Odoo-compatible `__custom__.group_<id>` fallbacks, persist missing custom group XML IDs, and compute source fallback icons for approval/reject/return/cancel/cancel-workflow/draft/forward/transfer/action/email plus server-action print/Excel/cog cases. Base `ir.actions.server` now exposes `binding_type` in metadata and migrations for source icon parity. Frontend worker added required editable form-field UX for wizard/action dialogs: empty required fields block object RPC, mark invalid state, focus the field, and clear invalid state on input. Focused backend/base/migration/workflow/frontend tests passed. focused tests passed line 645
#450 aligned bounded OI workflow frontend action-menu metadata with source controller order. List toolbar actions and the local OI static list-controller asset now expose source keys/order/icons (`update_status` sequence 100 `fa fa-code`, `approve` sequence 110 `fa fa-thumbs-up`, `approve_log` sequence 120 `fa fa-arrows-h`), while preserving the backend `action_approve_all` RPC. Form workflow actions expose source keys/icons with `approval_log` before `update_status` at sequence 100. Added regression coverage for keys, sequence, icon classes, full list ordering, source static asset key parity, and source `WORKFLOW` statusbar gating only on the `state` field. Focused addon/frontend tests passed. focused tests passed line 646
#451 added bounded native statusbar click/write behavior. Statusbar widgets now render accessible button items, respect `options.clickable` and readonly attributes, disable the current item, write changed selection values through `orm.webSave`/`write`, refresh after save, and emit a workflow statusbar-update event. Regression coverage asserts selected-item no-op behavior, save specification shape, refresh/event dispatch, clickable disablement, source `WORKFLOW` gating only on `state`, and existing OI duration rendering. Focused frontend tests passed. focused tests passed line 647
#452 added bounded selected-record ActionMenus parity. The webclient now renders loaded `viewDescription.actionMenus` for list and form views, keeps OI static workflow actions in the source static group before server actions, renders print/action menu sections with Odoo-compatible classes/roles, enables list menu items from the selected record set, and executes server/print actions by ID with `active_id`, `active_ids`, `active_model`, and `active_domain` context plus close-refresh handling. Regression coverage asserts list server action/print execution, form server action execution, wrapper/menu section structure, selection gating, and preservation of workflow action metadata/order. Focused frontend tests passed. focused tests passed line 648
#453 added bounded backend toolbar binding parity for selected-record ActionMenus. `get_views` now populates source-shaped toolbar action/print payloads from persisted `ir.actions.act_window`, `ir.actions.act_url`, `ir.actions.server`, and `ir.actions.report` bindings by `binding_model_id`, filters `binding_type`, `binding_view_types`, groups, inactive server actions, and `res_model` read ACLs, returns empty toolbar maps when no bindings exist, and uses typed local action refs plus `/web/action/load` support to avoid per-table ID collisions. Reports now expose `binding_view_types` in model metadata, migrations, and legacy report loading defaults. Added HTTP/base/data/migration regressions. Focused backend tests and full CI passed. full CI passed line 649
#454 added bounded ActionMenus report-domain validation, source static actions, and domain-selected action context. The backend now implements `ir.actions.report.get_valid_action_reports` for numeric and local typed report refs, safe-evaluates stored report domains, filters against selected record IDs, and exposes generic `action_archive`, `action_unarchive`, and `export_data` call_kw methods. The webclient now validates print actions with report domains before execution, resolves domain-selected `active_ids` through `orm.search` with `active_ids_limit`, passes real `active_domain`, and renders source-ordered static list/form actions for active-field views (`export`, `archive`, `unarchive`, `delete`) with ORM/unlink/refresh/event handling. Added HTTP and frontend regressions. Focused tests and full CI passed. full CI passed line 650
#455 added bounded duplicate/copy and export download parity for ActionMenus. Backend call_kw now supports Odoo-shaped `copy` returning copied ID lists, uses source-like copy filtering for stored non-computed fields with many2many link preservation and `res.partner` copy-name suffixing, and adds guarded `/web/export/get_fields`, `/web/export/csv`, and real minimal `/web/export/xlsx` routes with form-data `data` payloads, CSV output, XLSX zip package output, and `base.group_allow_export` enforcement when that group is loaded. Frontend static list/form actions now include source-ordered Duplicate (`sequence=30`, `fa fa-clone`) and list Export uses a source-shaped `/web/export/csv` download payload with `ids`, `domain`, `fields`, `context`, and `import_compat`. Added HTTP and frontend regressions for copy, export fields, CSV, XLSX, list duplicate/export, and form duplicate. Focused tests and full CI passed. full CI passed line 651
#456 added source-shaped lazy print loading for ActionMenus. Print items now load when the Print toggle opens, domain-bound reports validate in one `ir.actions.report.get_valid_action_reports` call before rendering, non-domain reports are accepted immediately, invalid reports are hidden, and an Odoo-style disabled `No report available.` row renders when validation leaves no print items. Domain-selected print validation now resolves active-domain IDs before report validation, and list selection changes clear loaded print rows to prevent stale executions. Added frontend regressions for lazy loading, invalid-domain fallback, valid-domain execution, domain-selected validation IDs, and stale menu clearing. Focused frontend tests and full CI passed. full CI passed line 652
#457 added bounded global `ir.actions` identity parity. Concrete action models now allocate through synchronized global `ir.actions.actions` IDs, keep base rows in sync on create/write/unlink, reject explicit-ID cross-type collisions, and preserve legacy typed refs as transitional input. `/web/action/load` now resolves numeric global IDs through the global base type, keeps registry path/XMLID behavior for act-window actions, and falls back to persisted external IDs for URL/client/report/server actions. Toolbars now emit numeric global action IDs, report validation accepts numeric IDs, and stored URL/client actions load by numeric or XML ID. Added record and HTTP regressions for unique global IDs, write/unlink sync, collision rejection, numeric toolbar IDs, numeric report validation, global action load, and URL/client XML ID load. Full CI passed. full CI passed line 653
#458 aligned `/web/action/load` payloads with Odoo 19 readable-field filtering. Concrete action loads now merge synchronized global `ir.actions.actions` common fields, emit `display_name`, serialize Many2one/x2many fields into web-readable values, restrict each action type to its source readable field set, and support `ir.actions.act_window_close` by numeric global ID, XML ID, and concrete ref. Added missing readable metadata columns for act-window close-on-download, URL close, and report target/context/data/close-on-download. Server/report/window/URL/client/close action regressions now assert exact allowed key sets and forbidden implementation fields. Full CI passed. full CI passed line 654
#459 added Odoo 19 action default and `clean_action` parity. Concrete action create/default_get now applies Odoo-shaped defaults for act-window, URL, server, report, client, and base action metadata, including server `usage`, `evaluation_type`, update defaults, report binding/report type, URL/client targets, and act-window context/view/cache defaults. Base metadata and migrations now include server action `type` plus persisted default clauses for action tables. `call_button` dict returns now pass through Odoo-style readable-field filtering while preserving custom non-model payload keys. Added record/base/migration/http regressions for create/default_get defaults, cleaned report action leaks, and custom close-action keys. Full CI passed. full CI passed line 655
#460 added bounded action context/run/view parity. `/web/action/load` now applies request context through a request-scoped env and uses `active_id` to compute visible persisted `embedded_action_ids` for window actions. Direct cleaned act-window dictionaries now generate missing `views` from `view_id`/`view_mode`, and registry action payloads honor single-mode `ViewID`. Added bounded `/web/action/run` backed by the runtime server-action registry, with active record context propagation and cleaned returned action dictionaries. Added HTTP/runtime regressions and full CI passed. full CI passed line 656
#461 closed bounded frontend server-action execution parity. The webclient default action chain now dispatches `ir.actions.server` through `/web/action/run`, merges action context with active action-menu context, re-enters normal action execution for returned actions, converts falsey server-action results to `ir.actions.act_window_close`, and preserves original server-action `path` on returned actions. Backend server-action results now have an explicit `Result.Action` channel with metadata fallbacks retained. Added frontend service/action-menu regressions plus backend explicit-action regression. Full CI passed. full CI passed line 657
#462 added bounded Odoo-shaped server-action warning JSON-RPC parity. `/web/action/run` now maps `actions.ErrActionWarning` to HTTP 200 JSON-RPC errors with Odoo's `code: 0`, `message: "Odoo Server Error"`, exception name `odoo.addons.base.models.ir_actions.ServerActionWithWarningsError`, arguments, context, and debug fields. #463 added bounded Odoo singleton server-action iteration parity. Source-singleton action kinds (`object_create`, `object_write`, `object_copy`, `webhook`, `multi`, and `next_activity`) now split multi-record `active_ids` into per-record executions with rewritten `active_id`/`active_ids` context; source-multi kinds remain batched. `/web/action/run` now gives `active_ids` precedence over `active_id`. #464 added bounded Odoo report/binary `bin_size` parity. `RecordSet.Read` now honors `bin_size` and `bin_size_<field>` for `field.Binary`, `WebRead` inherits the conversion, `ir.attachment.datas` reports decoded base64 size with Odoo `human_size` formatting, dataset read/search/web-read paths apply request context before serialization, and `/web/action/load` forces `bin_size=true` for persisted report actions even when the request context says false. #465 added Odoo direct act-window `view_id + multi view_mode` validation parity. Direct cleaned action dictionaries now return an error when `views` is absent, `view_id` is set, and `view_mode` contains multiple modes, matching Odoo's non-db action guard; call-button, accounting-button, and server-action run paths now propagate clean-action errors instead of silently returning malformed actions. Single-mode numeric and many2one-shaped `view_id` generation remains supported, and frontend direct action normalization now preserves single-mode `view_id` while rejecting invalid multi-mode dictionaries before RPC/view loading. #466 added Odoo 19 physical action table-name parity. #467 closed bounded menu/button XMLID resolution for non-window actions. Runtime menu payloads now re-emit XMLIDs for all menu action models. `/web/action/load` and report validation resolve typed XMLID refs plus unique unqualified XMLID names for action buttons. The webclient now renders and executes form `type="action"` buttons, normalizes `%(... )d/s`, direct XMLIDs, and numeric refs, passes active context, and wires started default services through the server-action executor. Missing currency or missing rate falls back to divisor `1`. #504 closed bounded Odoo 19 search-order handling. Record-layer `SearchWithOptions` now applies model `_order` by default, parses comma-separated `order` terms with optional `asc`/`desc`, `nulls first`/`nulls last`, and syntactic `:func`, orders before offset/limit, delegates bare many2one ordering to the comodel `_order`, preserves raw FK ordering for `.id`, supports property-field paths, and rejects arbitrary many2one dotted paths plus x2many order fields. HTTP `/web/dataset/call_kw` now supports generic `search` with positional/keyword `offset`, `limit`, and `order`; existing `search_read` and `web_search_read` now become effective through the record layer; AI search ordering now works; frontend ORM tests prove order kwargs are preserved. Added record, HTTP, AI runtime, and frontend regressions for default order, explicit multi-term order, null placement, many2one comodel/raw-id ordering, paging after sort, invalid semantic order, generic `search`, ordered `search_read`, ordered `web_search_read`, and AI `search`. Source/local audit agents were closed. Affected package tests passed. package tests passed line 700
#505 closed bounded Odoo 19 nested x2many `web_read` spec parity. Record-layer `WebRead` now expands one2many/many2many specs with recursive `fields`, per-field `context`, `order`, and source-shaped `limit` behavior: related IDs are ordered first, only the limited prefix is fetched recursively, and remaining relation entries are returned as `{"id": id}` placeholders. Empty x2many specs continue returning raw ID lists; nested `domain` and `offset` remain ignored to match source server behavior. Added record regressions for one2many ID-only reads, recursive child fields, nested many2one formatting, binary `bin_size` context, ignored nested domain/offset, ordering before limit, placeholders, many2many ordering/limit placeholders, and invalid nested order errors plus HTTP JSON-RPC coverage. Source/local/OI audit agents were closed. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 701
#506 added bounded Odoo 19 inbound bounce processing parity. Outbound queue sends now assign/store RFC `Message-Id` on `mail.mail` and `mail.message`, inbound `mail.thread.message_process` parses RFC822 delivery-status bounces, detects bounce alias/mailer-daemon/multipart-report signals, extracts `Final-Recipient` and original `Message-Id`/Microsoft original IDs, updates matching `mail.notification` rows to `notification_status='bounce'`, `failure_type='mail_bounce'`, stores plaintext failure reasons, increments/resets partner `message_bounce`, and exposes `res.partner.email_normalized` plus `mail.message.message_id` in models/migrations. Added mail, HTTP, base, and migration regressions. Affected package tests passed. package tests passed line 702
#507 added bounded non-bounce `mail.thread.message_process` routing parity. Inbound processing now normalizes/generates RFC `Message-Id`, skips duplicates by stored `mail.message.message_id`, resolves reply parents through `In-Reply-To` then the last 32 `References`, posts replies to the parent business record with `parent_id`, preserves inbound `From`, subject, date, HTML/plain body, resolved author/recipient partners, stores inbound `message_id`, returns routed thread IDs through `/web/dataset/call_kw`, supports fallback model creation with Odoo-like `name`/`email` defaults, and supports constrained active `mail.alias` local/full-email routing to new records. Added mail and HTTP regressions for reply routing, duplicate delivery, fallback creation, simple alias creation, author lookup, stored message IDs, and call_kw return IDs. Affected package tests passed. package tests passed line 703
#508 added bounded Odoo 19 inbound MIME attachment and original-email ingestion parity. `mail.thread.message_process` now collects explicit and inline MIME attachments, decodes base64/quoted-printable payloads, creates `ir.attachment` rows owned by the routed business record, links them through `mail.message.attachment_ids`, rewrites inline `cid:` image references to `/web/image/<id>?access_token=...`, supports `save_original` by attaching `original_email.eml`, and honors `strip_attachments` through direct mail processing and HTTP `call_kw`. Added mail and HTTP regressions for file attachments, inline CID rewrite, original EML preservation, and strip behavior. Source/local/fetchmail audit agents were closed. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 704
#509 added bounded Odoo 19 fetchmail polling parity. `fetchmail.server` now has an injectable POP/IMAP connector path, real confirm-login checks, ordered confirmed-server polling, 50-message default batch limits, per-message inbound processing through `mail.thread.message_process`, handled-message marking after processing failures, success/error date updates, 5-day repeated-failure deactivation, and password redaction in stored errors. HTTP `button_confirm_login` and `fetch_mail` route through the fetchmail service, runtime registers `mail.fetchmail` and `fetchmail.server._fetch_mails`, and base data seeds the inactive 5-minute fetchmail cron. #510 closed bounded Odoo 19 inbound mail metadata parity. `mail.message` now exposes source-shaped gateway fields (`incoming_email_to`, `incoming_email_cc`, `outgoing_email_to`, `reply_to`, `reply_to_force_new`, `mail_server_id`, `email_layout_xmlid`, `email_add_signature`) in model metadata, static SQL, and versioned migrations. Inbound `message_process` now stores Odoo-style filtered To/Cc metadata by aggregating Delivered-To/To/Cc headers and excluding active alias addresses, ignores sender-controlled inbound `Reply-To`, and computes stored `reply_to` from catchall/default fallback semantics. Fetchmail reuses the same metadata path. Added direct mail, HTTP, fetchmail, base, and migration regressions. Source/local metadata audit agents were closed. `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 706
#511 closed bounded Odoo 19 inbound loop detection parity. Inbound `message_process` now ignores replies to Odoo loop-detection bounce references before routing, keeps generic auto-response headers non-blocking, resolves routes before mutation, blocks sender-volume loops before creating records/attachments/messages, supports `mail.gateway.allowed`, reads `mail.gateway.loop.minutes` and `mail.gateway.loop.threshold`, emits an outgoing loop-detection bounce mail with tagged references, and lets HTTP/fetchmail surface loop ignores as non-error `false`/processed results. #514 closed bounded Odoo 19 inbound `mail.alias` contact-security parity. Alias routing now resolves authors before route selection, enforces `alias_contact` policies per matched candidate, skips denied candidates when a later recipient alias is allowed, rejects unknown policies fail-closed, requires known partners for `partners`, and requires target or alias-parent followers for `followers`. Rejections do not create target records, attachments, or `mail.message` rows and do not fall back to caller fallback models. #515 closed bounded Odoo 19 inbound alias rejection bounce/status parity. Alias denial now creates an outgoing `mail.mail` bounce with source-shaped `MAILER-DAEMON` sender selection, `Re:` subject, original RFC reference, auto-delete notification semantics, default or custom `alias_bounced_content` body, and quoted inbound body without creating an inbound target, attachment, or `mail.message`. Config-style alias errors now mark `alias_status='invalid'`; successful alias-created records mark aliases `valid`; access-policy rejections leave existing status unchanged; denied aliases skipped in favor of a later allowed alias do not bounce. Added direct mail and HTTP regressions for partners rejection bounce, followers missing-record config bounce, custom bounced content, skipped-denial no-bounce behavior, invalid policy status, and HTTP `message_process` bounce/status observability. Template and direct compose generation now maintain a campaign-scoped normalized recipient ledger and use existing non-canceled `mailing.trace` rows to suppress duplicate recipients before `mail.message`/`mail.mail` creation, creating trace-only canceled rows with `failure_type='mail_dup'`. Queue delivery now cancels later queued duplicate mass-mail rows as `mail_dup` before SMTP handoff by comparing normalized recipients against earlier trace-linked mail rows. Added template-generation and queue regressions for duplicate trace-only cancellation, preserved first delivery, canceled later queue rows, and trace/mail state transitions. Source/local auditors were closed. Focused duplicate tests, full mail package tests, affected package tests, and full CI passed. full CI passed line 737
#542 closed bounded Odoo 19 mass-mailing generation-time missing/invalid/opt-out cancellation parity. Template, direct compose, and scheduled compose now apply source ordering before creating active rows: blacklist, missing recipient, invalid recipient, list opt-out, then duplicate. Missing and invalid recipients create trace-only canceled `mailing.trace` rows with `mail_email_missing` or `mail_email_invalid`, preserving raw invalid trace emails; selected-list opt-out creates trace-only `mail_optout` rows using the same opt-out/opt-in override semantics as queue delivery. Scheduled mass-mailing compose now skips active `mail.scheduled.message` rows for canceled recipients. Added focused regressions for template missing, template invalid, template list opt-out, direct invalid, and scheduled invalid cancellation plus no `mail.mail`/`mail.message`/scheduled active rows. Source/local auditors were closed. Focused tests, full mail package tests, affected package tests, and full CI passed. full CI passed line 738
#543 added bounded Odoo 19 `mailing.mailing` email send service and recipient selection. `mailing.mailing` now exposes send-state, scheduling, sender/reply, server, attachment, archive, subject, and domain fields in metadata/static SQL/versioned migrations. Added `SendMassMailings` and `ProcessMassMailingQueue` service paths that select recipients from list subscriptions or bounded stored `id in [...]` domains, exclude records already represented by `mailing.trace`, generate mass-mail `mail.mail`/`mailing.trace` rows through the existing composer pipeline, mark direct sends done with `sent_date`/`kpi_mail_required`, and mark queued no-recipient mailings done without error. HTTP `call_kw` now handles `mailing.mailing.action_send_mail`, `_action_send_mail`, and `_process_mass_mailing_queue`. Added mail service regressions for list-contact sends, existing-trace exclusion, queued no-recipient completion, and HTTP action dispatch. Source/local auditors were closed. Focused tests, affected package tests, and full CI passed. full CI passed line 739
#544 closed bounded Odoo 19 `mailing.mailing` queue/schedule/cancel/retry_failed button parity. Added `schedule_type` metadata/schema/migration coverage, `QueueMassMailings`, `ScheduleMassMailings`, `CancelMassMailings`, and `RetryFailedMassMailings`. `action_put_in_queue`/`action_launch` now set `state='in_queue'`; `action_schedule` queues future `schedule_date` mailings and otherwise returns a minimal schedule wizard action; `action_cancel` resets to draft, clears `schedule_date`, and sets `schedule_type='now'`; `action_retry_failed` removes failed `mail.mail` rows and linked `mailing.trace` rows before requeueing. HTTP `call_kw` dispatch covers all four buttons. Added mail and HTTP regressions for future scheduling, cancel reset, schedule wizard fallback, failed-mail cleanup, failed-trace cleanup, and requeue state. Focused tests, affected package tests, and full CI passed. full CI passed line 740
#545 closed bounded Odoo 19 Python literal `mailing_domain` recipient selection parity. Added domain parser regressions plus mail send regressions for Python tuples, tuple RHS `in`, prefix OR, invalid domains, blank/False no-recipient safety, existing-trace exclusion, and list-domain filtering. Mass-mailing list sends now support stored `list_ids` domains while preserving subscription-based base audience intersection. #576 closed bounded Odoo 19 DB-backed inbound duplicate message locking parity. Source audit confirmed `mail.thread.message_process` checks `mail.message.message_id`, uses PostgreSQL `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(message_id))` to treat concurrent processors as duplicates, returns `False` without error on duplicates, and keeps `mail.message.message_id` non-unique. Local inbound processing now supports an explicit `InboundMessageIDLocker` with a PostgreSQL transaction advisory-lock implementation, passes the provider through HTTP `message_process`, runtime fetchmail cron, and fetchmail options, and uses a `mail.inbound.message.lock` row/table as the default shared local-store lock so separate worker namespaces cannot both route the same inbound `Message-Id`. Base metadata, static SQL, and migrations now include `mail.inbound.message.lock` with a unique `message_id` index. Added regressions for external-lock busy duplicate handling, cross-worker inbound duplicate locking, cross-worker fetchmail duplicate marking, base metadata, and migration/static SQL coverage. Source/local/regression agents were closed. Focused lock tests, affected package tests, and full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work was skipped. full CI passed line 772
#577 revalidated requested Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity after the inbound-lock slice. Source/local/regression agents confirmed `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_message_id>` already matches Odoo 19 enterprise behavior: public 301 redirect, no bot suppression, no explicit method restriction, valid-code/missing-message plain-click behavior, IP/country/WhatsApp click metadata, campaign fallback from linked WhatsApp marketing traces, `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime`, mirrored marketing trace click timestamps, and `whatsapp_click` child scheduling/cancellation. Added a generated-link bridge regression in `internal/runtime/bootstrap_test.go` proving runtime-generated WhatsApp body/button tracked links route through the local HTTP controller, preserve generic tracker `short_url`, create click rows, increment counts, and update WhatsApp/marketing trace click timestamps. Focused HTTP/base/migration/runtime tests, uncached full runtime package, and full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work was skipped. full CI passed line 773
#578 rechecked Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity on the requested scope: source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. Source agent confirmed `enterprise/marketing_automation_whatsapp` exposes public `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_id>` with no bot guard, click creation through `link.tracker.click.whatsapp_message_id`, campaign lookup from linked WhatsApp marketing traces, `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime`, and `whatsapp_click` child-trace side effects. Local regression agent mapped existing coverage for parser edges, all-method WhatsApp redirects, missing/zero message IDs, campaign fallback, bot behavior, generated body/button links, and comparator mail/SMS tracking routes. Local record parity now derives `marketing.trace.links_click_datetime` from linked `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime` for WhatsApp traces, including traces created after a click and searches by the derived timestamp. Added a record regression for that computed-field parity. Focused HTTP/mail/runtime/base/migration/record tests and full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work was skipped for phase 2. full CI passed line 774
#579 closed bounded Odoo 19 fetchmail server-lock and per-message rollback parity while keeping accounting feature work deferred to phase 2. Source/local/regression agents confirmed Odoo 19 locks each `fetchmail.server` with `FOR NO KEY UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, revalidates the server domain after lock acquisition, processes messages in isolated transactions, rolls back failed message side effects, and still marks failed remote messages handled. Local fetchmail now has a `FetchmailServerLocker` interface, default process-local skip-locked behavior, PostgreSQL row-lock implementation, server revalidation after lock, HTTP/runtime locker wiring, and `record.Env` snapshot/restore rollback around each inbound message. Existing duplicate-message tests now isolate message-id lock behavior from server locks, and new regressions cover locked-server skipping, concurrent workers not double-fetching the same server, and continuing a batch after a failed message with side effects rolled back. Focused mail tests, affected mail/http/runtime tests, and full `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 775
#580 revalidated requested Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. Source/local agents confirmed `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_id>` is public, has default unsafe-method CSRF behavior, records `link.tracker.click.whatsapp_message_id` plus IP/country/campaign metadata, updates `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime`, mirrors linked `marketing.trace.links_click_datetime`, and handles `whatsapp_click`/`whatsapp_not_click` child traces. Local HTTP, runtime link generation, base metadata, migrations, and record computed-field coverage already match the requested route; no WhatsApp code change was required. Fixed a CI-blocking digest KPI regression by making the connected-user margin fixture use deterministic `res.users.login_date` buckets and by reading `mail.message.subtype_id` before filtering message subtypes. Source/local agents were closed. Focused WhatsApp HTTP, runtime, record, base/migration, and digest tests passed. Full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work was skipped for phase 2. full CI passed line 776
#581 revalidated the requested Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. Source agents confirmed `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_id>` is a public HTTP route with default Odoo CSRF behavior: `GET`, `HEAD`, `OPTIONS`, and `TRACE` reach the handler, while unsafe methods require a valid CSRF token before side effects. Local agents confirmed existing Go metadata, SQL schema, migrations, HTTP handling, runtime tracked-link generation, record-derived `marketing.trace.links_click_datetime`, and focused regressions already match the requested route; no runtime code change was required. Closed agents, ran focused WhatsApp HTTP/runtime/record/base/migration/dashboard checks, and full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. full CI passed line 777
#582 rechecked the requested Odoo 19 WhatsApp tracked-link route parity for source route/model fields, local metadata/HTTP handling, focused regressions, and CI. Source and local agents confirmed `/r/<code>/w/<whatsapp_id>` matches Odoo 19 enterprise behavior: public HTTP route, no bot suppression, default unsafe-method CSRF behavior, valid-code click creation before redirect resolution, plain-click behavior for missing WhatsApp messages, IP/country/campaign/WhatsApp click metadata, `whatsapp.message.links_click_datetime`, derived `marketing.trace.links_click_datetime`, and `whatsapp_click` / `whatsapp_not_click` child-trace effects. Local route, parser, metadata, SQL schema, migrations, runtime tracked-link generation, and existing focused regressions already match this slice; no runtime code change was required. Focused WhatsApp HTTP, runtime, record, base, migration, dashboard tests, and full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. full CI passed line 778
#583 closed bounded Odoo 19 SMS opt-out mailing-list edge parity. Source/local agents confirmed `/sms/<mailing_id>/<trace_code>` and `/sms/<mailing_id>/unsubscribe/<trace_code>` sanitize entry numbers, match SMS traces, opt out matching mailing-list subscriptions without adding phone blacklist rows, add/reactivate `phone.blacklist` only for non-list mailings, render public list names, mask private list names as `Mailing List`, and use the source fallback success text when no opted-out list is found. Local HTTP now returns actual matched subscription list IDs from phone opt-out handling, renders source-shaped list/fallback success bodies, and avoids leaking configured-but-unmatched list names. Added focused regressions for public/private actual-list display, unmatched configured list suppression, outside subscription non-mutation, list-mode no-match fallback, and no phone-blacklist side effects in list mode. Source/local/regression/dashboard agents were closed. Focused SMS tests, affected HTTP package tests, dashboard generator tests, and full `make ci` passed. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. full CI passed line 779
#584 made the Go server directly testable from `/web` and closed bounded Odoo 19 fetchmail admin notification/time-budget seconds semantics. `/web` now serves a local testing shell with enterprise-like/standard theme switch, health/session/module/menu cards, model browsing, raw RPC, demo partner creation, and automatic local dev login using the seeded `admin` user when protected endpoints require a session. Source/local agents confirmed fetchmail uses Odoo-shaped cron deadline extension, stale failure deactivation, and admin-channel notification; local code now wires `CronEndTime`, budget seconds, admin notification, and focused regressions. Focused fetchmail mail/runtime regressions, affected package suite, full HTTP package tests, and browser smoke checks passed. Full `make ci` was not rerun after the testing shell change. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. regression coverage added line 780
#585 closed bounded Odoo 19 model-specific mail-thread suggested-recipient and reply-all hook parity while keeping accounting feature work deferred to phase 2. Local mail recipient resolution now has source-shaped hook metadata for `res.partner` self-partner suggestions, `hr.employee` partner fields and `work_email`, selected source primary-email overrides, selected `mail.thread.cc` models, reply-all subtype filtering against `mail.mt_comment` plus model creation subtypes, and partner-email creation gating on manager/system access for both `/mail/message/post` and `/mail/partner/from_email`. Added focused regressions for model partner/primary-email hooks, registered CC-only behavior, reply-all subtype filtering, `partner_emails` manager gating, and direct partner-from-email manager gating. Focused HTTP recipient tests and the full HTTP package passed. Full `make ci` was not rerun. regression coverage added line 781
#586 made `/web` usable for immediate platform testing beyond the raw model browser. The live shell now shows app/menu cards from `/web/webclient/load_menus`, opens menu actions into the model browser, shows installable module rows from `ir.module.module`, supports a basic install-state write for uninstalled modules, fixes the installed module count from `/web/session/modules`, and adds row-level Open buttons with a reload/save record panel. The theme selector remains on `/web` as the active theme switch. Focused web route tests passed; full HTTP package and full `make ci` were not rerun after this UI-only shell change. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. focused tests passed line 782
#587 replaced the generic `/web` test-shell look with an Odoo-like webclient theme for immediate testing. `/web` now uses an Odoo-style purple enterprise top bar, app launcher icon, main nav, global record search, user pill, left app/module rail, status strip, app grid, record list view, record open/save panel, Apps install board, and developer RPC area without shell copy. Browser verification confirmed the page at `http://localhost:8069/web` renders the webclient surface, app cards, install app cards, record rows, and no `Testing shell` text. Focused web route tests and the full HTTP package passed. Full `make ci` was not rerun. Accounting feature work remains skipped for phase 2. not recorded line 783
#588 advanced the UI parity master goal for `/web` against Odoo 19 Enterprise reference evidence while keeping accounting skipped for phase 2. Parallel agents audited current UI, probed the local Odoo 19 reference, and patched disjoint launcher/navbar/control-panel/renderer surfaces. `/web` now has a full-height dark Enterprise-style launcher, no broken app icon glyphs, Apps catalog routing through the real Technical Apps menu when present, launcher search aliases for install/modules/developer/configuration, active app state in the navbar, icon-only view switch and pager controls, dialog aria/Escape handling, Odoo-like list/mobile card classes, and a bounded form sheet/statusbar layout. Release root detection now checks the executable directory so extracted production archives can verify module data without falling back to local compile-time source paths. Added `reports/uiux/ui_parity_master_20260621_final.md`, refreshed UI smoke screenshots, and ran focused frontend/runtime/HTTP checks plus 18/18 local visual smoke. not recorded line 784
#589 added a bounded non-accounting `ir.module.module` lifecycle service instead of direct state writes. The new service backs Odoo-style button methods for install, immediate install, upgrade marking, immediate upgrade, uninstall, immediate uninstall, and cancel states; installs dependencies in manifest order, rejects unknown/non-installable modules, blocks uninstall when installed dependents still require the module, protects `base`, and returns a reload client action with changed modules. `/web/session/modules` now reflects persisted `ir.module.module.state` instead of reporting every manifest as installed. Focused module lifecycle tests, module package tests, targeted HTTP call_kw regressions, and full HTTP package tests passed. package tests passed line 785
#590 advanced bounded clean-room `/web` UI parity for navigation, Apps catalog, control-panel icons, and Server Action form v1 while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The TypeScript navbar now swaps root apps for active Settings/Technical section menus, preserves the active app brand, and highlights nested topbar sections. The launcher now includes a dismissible registration notice, the Apps catalog sidebar/detail layout remains visible inside the webclient, view switch and pager controls render compact icon-style controls, readonly selection fields render Odoo-style pills, editable selection fields render radio pills when choices are known, `ir.actions.server.state` exposes explicit selection metadata through runtime `fields_get`, Server Action forms now include a clean-room header/meta band, Code/Help notebook, readonly code viewer, and code-editor-style textarea, and Server Action list/form labels now hide raw technical field names while displaying selection values as human labels. Canonical browser smoke passed 26/26 scenarios with new Server Action band/notebook/code/selection assertions and missing root evidence for systray, groups notebook, Delegation one2many, mobile flow, Apps install/catalog/lifecycle. Focused frontend/base/http/smoke checks and full `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 786
#591 advanced bounded Odoo-style mobile list card parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Mobile list records now render as full clickable and keyboard-openable cards with title/header regions, state badges with human selection labels, no separate `Open` button, skipped empty secondary fields, and tighter Odoo-like card spacing/focus styling. Focused frontend tests, frontend build, smoke inventory tests, and focused browser smoke for default mobile Server Actions, technical mobile list, and Server Actions desktop list labels passed. not recorded line 787
#592 advanced Odoo-style form control panel parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Form views no longer expose list/search controls or search option toggles, while retaining breadcrumbs, form action buttons, and existing view-switch behavior. Focused frontend tests, frontend build, smoke inventory tests, and focused browser smoke for desktop Server Action form plus mobile Server Action form flow passed. not recorded line 788
#593 advanced bounded Odoo 19 date group-by search parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Search arch parsing now recognizes date filter metadata, normalizes bare date/datetime `group_by` descriptors to month, renders Odoo-style Year/Quarter/Month/Week/Day interval child menus, carries category/value labels into date group-by facets, and keeps fallback date group-by active checked state after rerender. Added focused parser/window-action tests and browser smoke for interval menu, year selection, selected state, and search menu/filter regression. not recorded line 789
#594 advanced bounded Odoo 19 date filter period search parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Search arch parsing now captures `date`, `default_period`, and start/end month/year metadata; the control panel renders generated current/previous month, quarter, and year period options; generated facets carry period/default-year metadata; month/quarter selection auto-adds the matching year; removing the last selected year clears generated periods; fallback date filters prefer business date fields and preserve checked state after rerender; generated date/datetime domains now use server-compatible flat prefix tokens. Added focused parser/search-model/window-action/control-panel tests and browser smoke for period menu rendering, current-month filtering, selected month/year state, rendered facets, and filtered rows. not recorded line 790
#595 advanced bounded Odoo 19 target-new dialog chrome parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Source/local agents confirmed Odoo 19 form dialogs suppress the normal control panel, start editable, render Save/Discard and XML footer buttons in the modal footer, disable action menus, keep list/kanban modal control-panel layouts, and constrain mobile/fullscreen dialog chrome. Local UI now applies those clean-room rules: form/settings dialog actions render body without control panel, footer buttons are extracted and rebound after rerender, modal forms start editable without Edit, target-new form action menus are disabled, list/kanban dialogs keep normal action layout, and desktop/mobile dialog height keeps the footer inside the viewport. Added focused dialog unit coverage and browser smoke for target-new settings dialog footer/body state plus desktop/mobile form regressions. regression coverage added line 791
#596 strict clean-room Odoo-like UI P0 gate for the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Launcher/topbar workers and main verification replaced shell-like launcher chrome with Odoo-named launcher/topbar contracts, fixed collapsed app icons and mobile launcher grid, restored fixed 46px topbar behavior, routed Apps to the Apps catalog instead of a generic module kanban, opened user preferences in an action dialog, added Users list/form fallback fields for poor source views, preserved target-new dialog footer parity, and added strict browser smoke gates for launcher, topbar, Apps lifecycle/detail, user preferences, Users list/form, kanban, automation, scheduled actions, dialogs, and mobile. Full visual smoke passed 36/36 and `make ci` passed after the fixes. full CI passed line 792
#597 tightened the strict Odoo-like UI gate after production showed Settings/action layout drift. Settings now renders Odoo-style app tabs, block grids, non-generic action labels (`Manage Users`, `Open Server Actions`, `Open Automated Actions`, `Open AI Apps`), active-tab switching, action-box metadata, compact legacy Settings buttons, SVG launcher wallpaper, no synthetic app-icon text, white action topbar with purple launcher dots, and mobile x2many relation metadata for Odoo-like card layout checks. Added strict local/prod smoke assertions for Settings grids/tab switching, Apps catalog icons, launcher wallpaper, topbar contract, legacy shell cues, and mobile one2many cards. Verified with focused frontend tests, strict local visual smoke, direct runtime group isolation, and full `make ci`. Accounting remains phase 2. not recorded line 793
#598 advanced bounded Odoo 19 custom filter dialog parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. The search dropdown now opens an Odoo-shaped custom filter dialog with field/operator/value controls, applies text facets through the action service, defaults Server Actions to the human `Model` field backed by `model_name`, displays `Model contains mail` without raw field identifiers, reruns filtered records, and restores the list when the facet is removed. Added focused frontend regression coverage plus a dedicated `default-custom-filter-dialog-desktop` browser smoke scenario. #599 advanced bounded Odoo 19 relation field lookup parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Many2one, many2many tag, and one2many child many2one editors now evaluate field `domain`, merge field `context` into action context, honor field/search `limit`, expose relation lookup metadata for smoke/UI checks, and send source-shaped `name_search` kwargs. Readonly many2one fields now honor `options.no_open` by rendering non-clickable values. Dotted relation-path domains are guarded out of autocomplete calls until backend relation-join matching exists, preventing empty dropdowns in current OI forms. Added frontend regressions for many2one, many2many, one2many relation lookup kwargs, no-open readonly rendering, and dotted-domain guard. Focused frontend tests, frontend build, focused HTTP name-search context checks, and full `make ci` passed. full CI passed line 795
#600 advanced bounded Odoo 19 relation autocomplete affordance parity in the default TypeScript `/web` client while keeping accounting deferred to phase 2. Many2one, one2many child many2one, and many2many tag editors now render Odoo-shaped `Search More...` rows, bounded expanded `name_search` requests, option-gated quick-create rows, option-gated Create/Edit dialog actions, and request-sequence guards so stale lookup responses cannot overwrite newer results. Added backend `name_create` call_kw support with simple-field validation and frontend quick-create now uses it instead of unsafe generic `create`. Existing `no_create`, `no_quick_create`, and `no_create_edit` options gate the new rows, while dotted-domain skip behavior remains preserved. Added focused frontend/backend regressions plus local browser smoke coverage for Server Action relation Search More. regression coverage added line 796
